A1-1.flavor_text: There is a plant seed on its back right from the day this Pokémon is born. The seed slowly grows larger.

A1-2.flavor_text: When the bulb on its back grows large, it appears to lose the ability to stand on its hind legs.

attacks.name: Mega Drain
attacks.effect: Heal 30 damage from this Pokémon.

A1-3.flavor_text: Its plant blooms when it is absorbing solar energy. It stays on the move to seek sunlight.

attacks.name: Giant Bloom
attacks.effect: Heal 30 damage from this Pokémon.

attacks.name: Find a Friend
attacks.effect: Put 1 random [G] Pokémon from your deck into your hand.

A1-5.flavor_text: For protection, it releases a horrible stench from the antenna on its head to drive away enemies.

A1-6.flavor_text: It is waiting for the moment to evolve. At this stage, it can only harden, so it remains motionless to avoid attack.

ability.name: Ability: Powder Heal
ability.effect: Once during your turn, you may heal 20 damage from each of your Pokémon.

A1-7.flavor_text: In battle, it flaps its wings at great speed to release highly toxic dust into the air.

A1-8.flavor_text: Often found in forests and grasslands. It has a sharp, toxic barb of around two inches on top of its head.

A1-9.flavor_text: Almost incapable of moving, this Pokémon can only harden its shell to protect itself when it is in danger.

A1-10.flavor_text: It has three poisonous stingers on its forelegs and its tail. They are used to jab its enemy repeatedly.

A1-11.flavor_text: If exposed to moonlight, it starts to move. It roams far and wide at night to scatter its seeds.

A1-12.flavor_text: Its pistils exude an incredibly foul odor. The horrid stench can cause fainting at a distance of 1.25 miles.

attacks.name: Soothing Scent
attacks.effect: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Asleep.

A1-13.flavor_text: It has the world’s largest petals. With every step, the petals shake out heavy clouds of toxic pollen.

A1-14.flavor_text: The mushrooms, known as tochukaso, are controlling the bug. Even if the bug bugs the mushrooms, they tell it to bug off.

A1-15.flavor_text: The bug is mostly dead, with the mushroom on its back having become the main body. If the mushroom comes off, the bug stops moving.

A1-16.flavor_text: Poison oozes from all over its body. It catches small bug Pokémon at night that are attracted by light.

attacks.name: Poison Powder
attacks.effect: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Poisoned.

A1-17.flavor_text: The wings are covered with dustlike scales. Every time it flaps its wings, it looses highly toxic dust.

A1-18.flavor_text: Even though its body is extremely skinny, it is blindingly fast when catching its prey.

A1-19.flavor_text: The leafy parts act as cutters for slashing foes. It spits a fluid that dissolves everything.

ability.name: Ability: Fragrance Trap
ability.effect: If this Pokémon is in the Active Spot, once during your turn, you may switch in 1 of your opponent's Benched Basic Pokémon to the Active Spot.

A1-20.flavor_text: Said to live in huge colonies deep in jungles, although no one has ever returned from there.

A1-21.flavor_text: Though it may look like it’s just a bunch of eggs, it’s a proper Pokémon. Exeggcute communicates with others of its kind via telepathy, apparently.

attacks.name: Stomp
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 more damage.

A1-22.flavor_text: Each of Exeggutor’s three heads is thinking different thoughts. The three don’t seem to be very interested in one another.

attacks.name: Tropical Swing
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 40 more damage.

attacks.name: Absorb
attacks.effect: Heal 10 damage from this Pokémon.

A1-24.flavor_text: Hidden beneath a tangle of vines that grows nonstop even if the vines are torn off, this Pokémon’s true appearance remains a mystery.

A1-25.flavor_text: It slashes through grass with its sharp scythes, moving too fast for the human eye to track.

attacks.name: Double Horn
attacks.effect: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 50 damage for each heads.

A1-26.flavor_text: These Pokémon judge one another based on pincers. Thicker, more impressive pincers make for more popularity with the opposite gender.

A1-27.flavor_text: It shoots cotton from its body to protect itself. If it gets caught up in hurricane-strength winds, it can get sent to the other side of the Earth.

A1-28.flavor_text: It scatters cotton all over the place as a prank. If it gets wet, it’ll become too heavy to move and have no choice but to answer for its mischief.

attacks.name: Blot
attacks.effect: Heal 10 damage from this Pokémon.

A1-29.flavor_text: The leaves on its head grow right back even if they fall out. These bitter leaves refresh those who eat them.

attacks.name: Leaf Supply
attacks.effect: Take a [G] Energy from your Energy Zone and attach it to 1 of your Benched [G] Pokémon.

A1-30.flavor_text: The fragrance of the garland on its head has a relaxing effect, but taking care of it is very difficult.

attacks.name: Surprise Attack
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.

A1-31.flavor_text: Until recently, people living in the mountains would ride on the backs of these Pokémon to traverse the mountain paths.

A1-32.flavor_text: It can sense the feelings of others by touching them with its horns. This species has assisted people with their work since 5,000 years ago.

attacks.name: Ember
attacks.effect: Discard a [R] Energy from this Pokémon.

A1-33.flavor_text: It has a preference for hot things. When it rains, steam is said to spout from the tip of its tail.

A1-34.flavor_text: It has a barbaric nature. In battle, it whips its fiery tail around and slashes away with sharp claws.

attacks.name: Fire Spin
attacks.effect: Discard 2 [R] Energy from this Pokémon.

A1-35.flavor_text: It spits fire that is hot enough to melt boulders. It may cause forest fires by blowing flames.

attacks.name: Crimson Storm
attacks.effect: Discard 2 [R] Energy from this Pokémon.

attacks.name: Tail Whip
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon can't attack during your opponent's next turn.

A1-37.flavor_text: While young, it has six gorgeous tails. When it grows, several new tails are sprouted.

attacks.name: Flamethrower
attacks.effect: Discard a [R] Energy from this Pokémon.

A1-38.flavor_text: It is said to live 1,000 years, and each of its tails is loaded with supernatural powers.

A1-39.flavor_text: It has a brave and trustworthy nature. It fearlessly stands up to bigger and stronger foes.

attacks.name: Heat Tackle
attacks.effect: This Pokémon also does 20 damage to itself.

A1-40.flavor_text: An ancient picture scroll shows that people were captivated by its movement as it ran through prairies.

attacks.name: Inferno Onrush
attacks.effect: This Pokémon also does 20 damage to itself.

A1-42.flavor_text: It can’t run properly when it’s newly born. As it races around with others of its kind, its legs grow stronger.

A1-43.flavor_text: This Pokémon can be seen galloping through fields at speeds of up to 150 mph, its fiery mane fluttering in the wind.

A1-44.flavor_text: Magmar dispatches its prey with fire. But it regrets this habit once it realizes that it has burned its intended prey to a charred crisp.

attacks.name: Flamethrower
attacks.effect: Discard 1 [R] Energy from this Pokémon.

A1-45.flavor_text: Inhaled air is carried to its flame sac, heated, and exhaled as fire that reaches over 3,000 degrees Fahrenheit.

attacks.name: Sky Attack
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.

A1-46.flavor_text: It’s one of the legendary bird Pokémon. When Moltres flaps its flaming wings, they glimmer with a dazzling red glow.

attacks.name: Inferno Dance
attacks.effect: Flip 3 coins. Take an amount of [R] Energy from your Energy Zone equal to the number of heads and attach it to your Benched [R] Pokémon in any way you like.

A1-48.flavor_text: There’s a hole in its tail that allows it to draw in the air it needs to keep its fire burning. If the hole gets blocked, this Pokémon will fall ill.

A1-49.flavor_text: It taunts its prey and lures them into narrow, rocky areas where it then sprays them with toxic gas to make them dizzy and take them down.

A1-50.flavor_text: Salazzle makes its opponents light-headed with poisonous gas, then captivates them with alluring movements to turn them into loyal servants.

A1-51.flavor_text: It stores flammable gas in its body and uses it to generate heat. The yellow sections on its belly get particularly hot.

attacks.name: Fire Blast
attacks.effect: Discard a [R] Energy from this Pokémon.

A1-52.flavor_text: When it heats up, its body temperature reaches about 1,500 degrees Fahrenheit. It lashes its body like a whip and launches itself at enemies.

A1-53.flavor_text: When it retracts its long neck into its shell, it squirts out water with vigorous force.

A1-54.flavor_text: It is recognized as a symbol of longevity. If its shell has algae on it, that Wartortle is very old.

attacks.name: Hydro Pump
attacks.effect: If this Pokémon has at least 2 extra [W] Energy attached, this attack does 60 more damage.

A1-55.flavor_text: It crushes its foe under its heavy body to cause fainting. In a pinch, it will withdraw inside its shell.

attacks.name: Hydro Bazooka
attacks.effect: If this Pokémon has at least 2 extra [W] Energy attached, this attack does 60 more damage.

attacks.name: Headache
attacks.effect: Your opponent can't use any Supporter cards from their hand during their next turn.

A1-57.flavor_text: It is constantly wracked by a headache. When the headache turns intense, it begins using mysterious powers.

A1-58.flavor_text: When it swims at full speed using its long, webbed limbs, its forehead somehow begins to glow.

A1-59.flavor_text: For Poliwag, swimming is easier than walking. The swirl pattern on its belly is actually part of the Pokémon’s innards showing through the skin.

A1-60.flavor_text: Staring at the swirl on its belly causes drowsiness. This trait of Poliwhirl’s has been used in place of lullabies to get children to go to sleep.

ability.name: Ability: Counterattack
ability.effect: If this Pokémon is in the Active Spot and is damaged by an attack from your opponent's Pokémon, do 20 damage to the Attacking Pokémon.

A1-61.flavor_text: Its body is solid muscle. When swimming through cold seas, Poliwrath uses its impressive arms to smash through drift ice and plow forward.

A1-62.flavor_text: Tentacool is not a particularly strong swimmer. It drifts across the surface of shallow seas as it searches for prey.

attacks.name: Poison Tentacles
attacks.effect: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Poisoned.

A1-63.flavor_text: When the red orbs on Tentacruel’s head glow brightly, watch out. The Pokémon is about to fire off a burst of ultrasonic waves.

A1-64.flavor_text: Thanks to its thick fat, cold seas don’t bother it at all, but it gets tired pretty easily in warm waters.

A1-65.flavor_text: It sunbathes on the beach after meals. The rise in its body temperature helps its digestion.

A1-66.flavor_text: It is encased in a shell that is harder than diamond. Inside, however, it is surprisingly tender.

ability.name: Ability: Shell Armor
ability.effect: This Pokémon takes -10 damage from attacks.

A1-67.flavor_text: Cloyster that live in seas with harsh tidal currents grow large, sharp spikes on their shells.

A1-68.flavor_text: It can be found near the sea. The large pincers grow back if they are torn out of their sockets.

attacks.name: KO Crab
attacks.effect: Flip 2 coins. If both of them are heads, this attack does 80 more damage.

A1-69.flavor_text: Its large and hard pincer has 10,000-horsepower strength. However, being so big, it is unwieldy to move.

A1-70.flavor_text: Horsea makes its home in oceans with gentle currents. If this Pokémon is under attack, it spits out pitch-black ink and escapes.

attacks.name: Water Arrow
attacks.effect: This attack does 50 damage to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon.

A1-71.flavor_text: It’s the males that raise the offspring. While Seadra are raising young, the spines on their backs secrete thicker and stronger poison.

A1-72.flavor_text: Its dorsal, pectoral, and tail fins wave elegantly in water. That is why it is known as the Water Dancer.

attacks.name: Horn Hazard
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.

A1-73.flavor_text: In autumn, its body becomes more fatty in preparing to propose to a mate. It takes on beautiful colors.

A1-74.flavor_text: If you visit a beach at the end of summer, you’ll be able to see groups of Staryu lighting up in a steady rhythm.

A1-75.flavor_text: This Pokémon has an organ known as its core. The organ glows in seven colors when Starmie is unleashing its potent psychic powers.

A1-77.flavor_text: An underpowered, pathetic Pokémon. It may jump high on rare occasions but never more than seven feet.

attacks.name: Hyper Beam
attacks.effect: Discard a random Energy from your opponent's Active Pokémon.

A1-78.flavor_text: Once it appears, it goes on a rampage. It remains enraged until it demolishes everything around it.

attacks.name: Hydro Pump
attacks.effect: If this Pokémon has at least 3 extra [W] Energy attached, this attack does 70 more damage.

A1-79.flavor_text: A smart and kindhearted Pokémon, it glides across the surface of the sea while its beautiful song echoes around it.

attacks.name: Bubble Drain
attacks.effect: Heal 30 damage from this Pokémon.

A1-80.flavor_text: It lives close to water. Its long tail is ridged with a fin, which is often mistaken for a mermaid’s.

A1-81.flavor_text: Because some Omanyte manage to escape after being restored or are released into the wild by people, this species is becoming a problem.

attacks.name: Ancient Whirlpool
attacks.effect: During your opponent's next turn, the Defending Pokémon can't attack.

A1-82.flavor_text: Weighed down by a large and heavy shell, Omastar couldn’t move very fast. Some say it went extinct because it was unable to catch food.

attacks.name: Ice Beam
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

A1-83.flavor_text: It’s said that this Pokémon’s beautiful blue wings are made of ice. Articuno flies over snowy mountains, its long tail fluttering along behind it.

attacks.name: Blizzard
attacks.effect: This attack also does 10 damage to each of your opponent's Benched Pokémon.

A1-85.flavor_text: When attacked, it uses its feathers to splash water, escaping under cover of the spray.

A1-86.flavor_text: Despite their elegant appearance, they can flap their wings strongly and fly for thousands of miles.

A1-87.flavor_text: It secretes flexible bubbles from its chest and back. The bubbles reduce the damage it would otherwise take when attacked.

A1-88.flavor_text: It can throw bubble-covered pebbles with precise control, hitting empty cans up to a hundred feet away.

ability.name: Ability: Water Shuriken
ability.effect: Once during your turn, you may do 20 damage to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon.

A1-89.flavor_text: It creates throwing stars out of compressed water. When it spins them and throws them at high speed, these stars can split metal in two.

A1-90.flavor_text: It lives in warm, shallow waters. If it encounters a foe, it will spit out its internal organs as a means to punch them.

attacks.name: Second Strike
attacks.effect: If your opponent's Active Pokémon has damage on it, this attack does 60 more damage.

A1-91.flavor_text: It grinds its teeth with great force to stimulate its brain. It fires the psychic energy created by this process from the protuberance on its head.

A1-92.flavor_text: It eats snow that has accumulated on the ground. It prefers soft, freshly fallen snow, so it will eat its way up a mountain, aiming for the peak.

attacks.name: Powder Snow
attacks.effect: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Asleep.

A1-93.flavor_text: Frosmoth senses air currents with its antennae. It sends its scales drifting on frigid air, making them fall like snow.

A1-94.flavor_text: When it is angered, it immediately discharges the energy stored in the pouches in its cheeks.

attacks.name: Thunderbolt
attacks.effect: Discard all Energy from this Pokémon.

A1-95.flavor_text: Its tail discharges electricity into the ground, protecting it from getting shocked.

attacks.name: Circle Circuit
attacks.effect: This attack does 30 damage for each of your Benched [L] Pokémon.

A1-97.flavor_text: The electromagnetic waves emitted by the units at the sides of its head expel antigravity, which allows it to float.

ability.name: Ability: Volt Charge
ability.effect: Once during your turn, you may take a [L] Energy from your Energy Zone and attach it to this Pokémon.

A1-98.flavor_text: Three Magnemite are linked by a strong magnetic force. Earaches will occur if you get too close.

A1-99.flavor_text: It rolls to move. If the ground is uneven, a sudden jolt from hitting a bump can cause it to explode.

A1-100.flavor_text: The more energy it charges up, the faster it gets. But this also makes it more likely to explode.

attacks.name: Thunder Punch
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 40 more damage. If tails, this Pokémon also does 20 damage to itself.

A1-101.flavor_text: Many power plants keep Ground-type Pokémon around as a defense against Electabuzz that come seeking electricity.

attacks.name: Pin Missile
attacks.effect: Flip 4 coins. This attack does 40 damage for each heads.

A1-102.flavor_text: It concentrates the weak electric charges emitted by its cells and launches wicked lightning bolts.

attacks.name: Raging Thunder
attacks.effect: This attack also does 30 damage to 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

A1-103.flavor_text: This Pokémon has complete control over electricity. There are tales of Zapdos nesting in the dark depths of pitch-black thunderclouds.

attacks.name: Thundering Hurricane
attacks.effect: Flip 4 coins. This attack does 50 damage for each heads.

A1-105.flavor_text: When thunderclouds cover the sky, it will appear. It can catch lightning with its mane and store the electricity.

attacks.name: Thunder Spear
attacks.effect: This attack does 30 damage to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon.

A1-106.flavor_text: When this ill-tempered Pokémon runs wild, it shoots lightning from its mane in all directions.

A1-107.flavor_text: While one alone doesn’t have much power, a chain of many Tynamo can be as powerful as lightning.

A1-108.flavor_text: They coil around foes and shock them with electricity-generating organs that seem simply to be circular patterns.

attacks.name: Thunder Fang
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

A1-109.flavor_text: They crawl out of the ocean using their arms. They will attack prey on shore and immediately drag it into the ocean.

A1-110.flavor_text: When spread, the frills on its head act like solar panels, generating the power behind this Pokémon’s electric moves.

attacks.name: Quick Attack
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 40 more damage.

A1-111.flavor_text: One Heliolisk basking in the sun with its frill outspread is all it would take to produce enough electricity to power a city.

attacks.name: Thunder Shock
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

A1-112.flavor_text: This Pokémon generates electricity when it digests food. It uses its five hard teeth to scrape seaweed off surfaces and eat it.

A1-113.flavor_text: It is said that happiness will come to those who see a gathering of Clefairy dancing under a full moon.

A1-114.flavor_text: A timid fairy Pokémon that is rarely seen, it will run and hide the moment it senses people.

attacks.name: Teleport
attacks.effect: Switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

A1-115.flavor_text: This Pokémon uses its psychic powers while it sleeps. The contents of Abra’s dreams affect the powers that the Pokémon wields.

A1-116.flavor_text: Using its psychic power, Kadabra levitates as it sleeps. It uses its springy tail as a pillow.

attacks.name: Psychic
attacks.effect: This attack does 30 more damage for each Energy attached to your opponent's Active Pokémon.

A1-117.flavor_text: It has an incredibly high level of intelligence. Some say that Alakazam remembers everything that ever happens to it, from birth till death.

A1-118.flavor_text: It is incredibly slow and dopey. It takes five seconds for it to feel pain when under attack.

A1-119.flavor_text: When a Slowpoke went hunting in the sea, its tail was bitten by a Shellder. That made it evolve into Slowbro.

A1-120.flavor_text: It wraps its opponent in its gas-like body, slowly weakening its prey by poisoning it through the skin.

A1-121.flavor_text: It likes to lurk in the dark and tap shoulders with a gaseous hand. Its touch causes endless shuddering.

attacks.name: Bother
attacks.effect: Your opponent can't use any Supporter cards from their hand during their next turn.

A1-122.flavor_text: To steal the life of its target, it slips into the prey’s shadow and silently waits for an opportunity.

ability.name: Ability: Shadowy Spellbind
ability.effect: As long as this Pokémon is in the Active Spot, your opponent can't use any Supporter cards from their hand.

A1-124.flavor_text: It remembers every dream it eats. It rarely eats the dreams of adults because children’s are much tastier.

ability.name: Ability: Sleep Pendulum
ability.effect: Once during your turn, you may flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Asleep.

A1-125.flavor_text: When it locks eyes with an enemy, it will use a mix of psi moves, such as Hypnosis and Confusion.

attacks.name: Barrier Attack
attacks.effect: During your opponent's next turn, this Pokémon takes -20 damage from attacks.

A1-126.flavor_text: The broadness of its hands may be no coincidence—many scientists believe its palms became enlarged specifically for pantomiming.

attacks.name: Psychic
attacks.effect: This attack does 20 more damage for each Energy attached to your opponent's Active Pokémon.

A1-127.flavor_text: Its strange cries sound like human language. There are some musicians who compose songs for Jynx to sing.

attacks.name: Power Blast
attacks.effect: Discard 2 [P] Energy from this Pokémon.

A1-128.flavor_text: It was created by a scientist after years of horrific gene-splicing and DNA-engineering experiments.

attacks.name: Psydrive
attacks.effect: Discard 2 [P] Energy from this Pokémon.

A1-130.flavor_text: The horns on its head provide a strong power that enables it to sense people’s emotions.

A1-131.flavor_text: It has a psychic power that enables it to distort the space around it and see into the future.

ability.name: Ability: Psy Shadow
ability.effect: Once during your turn, you may take 1 [P] Energy from your Energy Zone and attach it to the [P] Pokémon in the Active Spot.

A1-132.flavor_text: To protect its Trainer, it will expend all its psychic power to create a small black hole.

A1-133.flavor_text: While inside a cave, if you look up and see lots of heart-shaped marks lining the walls, it’s evidence that Woobat live there.

A1-134.flavor_text: Emitting powerful sound waves tires it out. Afterward, it won’t be able to fly for a little while.

A1-135.flavor_text: They were sculpted from clay in ancient times. No one knows why, but some of them are driven to continually line up boulders.

attacks.name: Double Lariat
attacks.effect: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 100 damage for each heads.

A1-136.flavor_text: Artillery platforms built into the walls of ancient castles served as perches from which Golurk could fire energy beams.

A1-137.flavor_text: It loves to bathe in the grit of dry, sandy areas. By sand bathing, the Pokémon rids itself of dirt and moisture clinging to its body.

A1-138.flavor_text: The drier the area Sandslash lives in, the harder and smoother the Pokémon’s spikes will feel when touched.

A1-139.flavor_text: It lives about one yard underground, where it feeds on plant roots. It sometimes appears aboveground.

attacks.name: Dig
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, during your opponent's next turn, prevent all damage from—and effects of—attacks done to this Pokémon.

A1-140.flavor_text: Its three heads bob separately up and down to loosen the soil nearby, making it easier for it to burrow.

A1-141.flavor_text: It lives in groups in the treetops. If it loses sight of its group, it becomes infuriated by its loneliness.

attacks.name: Fight Back
attacks.effect: If this Pokémon has damage on it, this attack does 60 more damage.

A1-142.flavor_text: It becomes wildly furious if it even senses someone looking at it. It chases anyone that meets its glare.

A1-143.flavor_text: Its whole body is composed of muscles. Even though it’s the size of a human child, it can hurl 100 grown-ups.

A1-144.flavor_text: Its muscular body is so powerful, it must wear a power-save belt to be able to regulate its motions.

A1-145.flavor_text: It quickly swings its four arms to rock its opponents with ceaseless punches and chops from all angles.

A1-147.flavor_text: Geodude that have lived a long life have had all their edges smoothed out until they’re totally round. They also have a calm, quiet disposition.

A1-148.flavor_text: It climbs up cliffs as it heads toward the peak of a mountain. As soon as it reaches the summit, it rolls back down the way it came.

attacks.name: Double-Edge
attacks.effect: This Pokémon also does 50 damage to itself.

A1-149.flavor_text: When Golem grow old, they stop shedding their shells. Those that have lived a long, long time have shells green with moss.

A1-150.flavor_text: As it digs through the ground, it absorbs many hard objects. This is what makes its body so solid.

attacks.name: Growl
attacks.effect: During your opponent's next turn, attacks used by the Defending Pokémon do -20 damage.

A1-151.flavor_text: When the memory of its departed mother brings it to tears, its cries echo mournfully within the skull it wears on its head.

A1-152.flavor_text: This Pokémon overcame its sorrow to evolve a sturdy new body. Marowak faces its opponents bravely, using a bone as a weapon.

attacks.name: Bonemerang
attacks.effect: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 80 damage for each heads.

attacks.name: Stretch Kick
attacks.effect: This attack does 30 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon.

A1-154.flavor_text: This amazing Pokémon has an awesome sense of balance. It can kick in succession from any position.

A1-155.flavor_text: Its punches slice the air. They are launched at such high speed, even a slight graze could cause a burn.

A1-156.flavor_text: Strong, but not too bright, this Pokémon can shatter even a skyscraper with its charging tackles.

A1-157.flavor_text: It begins walking on its hind legs after evolution. It can punch holes through boulders with its horn.

A1-158.flavor_text: This species is almost entirely extinct. Kabuto molt every three days, making their shells harder and harder.

attacks.name: Leech Life
attacks.effect: Heal from this Pokémon the same amount of damage you did to your opponent's Active Pokémon.

A1-159.flavor_text: Kabutops slices its prey apart and sucks out the fluids. The discarded body parts become food for other Pokémon.

A1-160.flavor_text: In one minute, a well-trained Mienfoo can chop with its arms more than 100 times.

A1-161.flavor_text: When Mienshao comes across a truly challenging opponent, it will lighten itself by biting off the fur on its arms.

A1-162.flavor_text: It’s very curious, but its means of investigating things is to try to punch them with its tentacles. The search for food is what brings it onto land.

attacks.name: Knock Back
attacks.effect: Switch out your opponent's Active Pokémon to the Bench. (Your opponent chooses the new Active Pokémon.)

A1-163.flavor_text: A body made up of nothing but muscle makes the grappling moves this Pokémon performs with its tentacles tremendously powerful.

A1-164.flavor_text: By dislocating its jaw, it can swallow prey larger than itself. After a meal, it curls up and rests.

attacks.name: Corner
attacks.effect: During your opponent's next turn, the Defending Pokémon can't retreat.

A1-165.flavor_text: The latest research has determined that there are over 20 possible arrangements of the patterns on its stomach.

attacks.name: Call for Family
attacks.effect: Put 1 random Nidoran♂ from your deck onto your Bench.

A1-166.flavor_text: Females are more sensitive to smells than males. While foraging, they’ll use their whiskers to check wind direction and stay downwind of predators.

A1-167.flavor_text: The horn on its head has atrophied. It’s thought that this happens so Nidorina’s children won’t get poked while their mother is feeding them.

attacks.name: Lovestrike
attacks.effect: This attack does 50 more damage for each of your Benched Nidoking.

A1-168.flavor_text: Nidoqueen is better at defense than offense. With scales like armor, this Pokémon will shield its children from any kind of attack.

A1-169.flavor_text: The horn on a male Nidoran’s forehead contains a powerful poison. This is a very cautious Pokémon, always straining its large ears.

A1-170.flavor_text: With a horn that’s harder than diamond, this Pokémon goes around shattering boulders as it searches for a moon stone.

attacks.name: Poison Horn
attacks.effect: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Poisoned.

A1-171.flavor_text: When it goes on a rampage, it’s impossible to control. But in the presence of a Nidoqueen it’s lived with for a long time, Nidoking calms down.

A1-172.flavor_text: It emits ultrasonic waves from its mouth to check its surroundings. Even in tight caves, Zubat flies around with skill.

A1-173.flavor_text: It loves to drink other creatures’ blood. It’s said that if it finds others of its kind going hungry, it sometimes shares the blood it’s gathered.

attacks.name: Poison Gas
attacks.effect: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Poisoned.

A1-174.flavor_text: Born from sludge, these Pokémon now gather in polluted places and increase the bacteria in their bodies.

attacks.name: Venoshock
attacks.effect: If your opponent's Active Pokémon is Poisoned, this attack does 50 more damage.

A1-175.flavor_text: It’s thickly covered with a filthy, vile sludge. It is so toxic, even its footprints contain poison.

A1-176.flavor_text: Its body is full of poisonous gas. It floats into garbage dumps, seeking out the fumes of raw, rotting trash.

ability.name: Ability: Gas Leak
ability.effect: Once during your turn, if this Pokémon is in the Active Spot, you may make your opponent's Active Pokémon Poisoned.

A1-177.flavor_text: If one of the twin Koffing inflates, the other one deflates. It constantly mixes its poisonous gases.

attacks.name: Crunch
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, discard a random Energy from your opponent's Active Pokémon.

A1-178.flavor_text: It uses its docile-looking face to lull foes into complacency, then bites with its huge, relentless jaws.

A1-179.flavor_text: Pawniard will fearlessly challenge even powerful foes. In a pinch, it will cling to opponents and pierce them with the blades all over its body.

A1-180.flavor_text: This Pokémon commands a group of several Pawniard. Groups that are defeated in territorial disputes are absorbed by the winning side.

attacks.name: Amass
attacks.effect: Take 1 [M] Energy from your Energy Zone and attach it to this Pokémon.

A1-181.flavor_text: It dissolves and eats metal. Circulating liquid metal within its body is how it generates energy.

ability.name: Ability: Hard Coat
ability.effect: This Pokémon takes -20 damage from attacks.

A1-182.flavor_text: At the end of its life-span, Melmetal will rust and fall apart. The small shards left behind will eventually be reborn as Meltan.

A1-183.flavor_text: It sheds many layers of skin as it grows larger. During this process, it is protected by a rapid waterfall.

A1-184.flavor_text: They say that if it emits an aura from its whole body, the weather will begin to change instantly.

attacks.name: Draco Meteor
attacks.effect: 1 of your opponent's Pokémon is chosen at random 4 times. For each time a Pokémon was chosen, do 50 damage to it.

A1-185.flavor_text: It is said that somewhere in the ocean lies an island where these gather. Only they live there.

A1-186.flavor_text: A common sight in forests and woods. It flaps its wings at ground level to kick up blinding sand.

A1-187.flavor_text: The claws on its feet are well developed. It can carry prey such as an Exeggcute to its nest over 60 miles away.

ability.name: Ability: Drive Off
ability.effect: Once during your turn, you may switch out your opponent's Active Pokémon to the Bench. (Your opponent chooses the new Active Pokémon.)

A1-188.flavor_text: When hunting, it skims the surface of water at high speed to pick off unwary prey such as Magikarp.

A1-189.flavor_text: Its incisors grow continuously throughout its life. If its incisors get too long, this Pokémon becomes unable to eat, and it starves to death.

A1-190.flavor_text: People say that it fled from its enemies by using its small webbed hind feet to swim from island to island in Alola.

A1-191.flavor_text: Its reckless nature leads it to stand up to others—even large Pokémon—if it has to protect its territory.

attacks.name: Drill Run
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, discard a random Energy from your opponent's Active Pokémon.

A1-192.flavor_text: Carrying food through Fearow’s territory is dangerous. It will snatch the food away from you in a flash!

A1-193.flavor_text: When its huge eyes waver, it sings a mysteriously soothing melody that lulls its enemies to sleep.

A1-194.flavor_text: It has a very fine fur. Take care not to make it angry, or it may inflate steadily and hit with a body slam.

attacks.name: Sleepy Song
attacks.effect: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Asleep.

attacks.name: Pay Day
attacks.effect: Draw 1 card.

A1-196.flavor_text: All it does is sleep during the daytime. At night, it patrols its territory with its eyes aglow.

attacks.name: Shadow Claw
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, discard a random card from your opponent's hand.

A1-197.flavor_text: Although its fur has many admirers, it is tough to raise as a pet because of its fickle meanness.

A1-198.flavor_text: The stalk this Pokémon carries in its wings serves as a sword to cut down opponents. In a dire situation, the stalk can also serve as food.

A1-199.flavor_text: A two-headed Pokémon that was discovered as a sudden mutation. It runs at a pace of over 60 miles per hour.

A1-200.flavor_text: An enemy that takes its eyes off any of the three heads—even for a second—will get pecked severely.

attacks.name: Continuous Lick
attacks.effect: Flip a coin until you get tails. This attack does 60 damage for each heads.

A1-201.flavor_text: If this Pokémon’s sticky saliva gets on you and you don’t clean it off, an intense itch will set in. The itch won’t go away, either.

A1-202.flavor_text: This kindly Pokémon lays highly nutritious eggs and shares them with injured Pokémon or people.

attacks.name: Dizzy Punch
attacks.effect: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 30 damage for each heads.

A1-203.flavor_text: Although it’s carrying its baby in a pouch on its belly, Kangaskhan is swift on its feet. It intimidates its opponents with quick jabs.

A1-204.flavor_text: When Tauros begins whipping itself with its tails, it’s a warning that the Pokémon is about to charge with astounding speed.

attacks.name: Copy Anything
attacks.effect: Choose 1 of your opponent's Pokémon's attacks and use it as this attack. If this Pokémon doesn't have the necessary Energy to use that attack, this attack does nothing.

A1-205.flavor_text: Its transformation ability is perfect. However, if made to laugh, it can’t maintain its disguise.

A1-206.flavor_text: Its ability to evolve into many forms allows it to adapt smoothly and perfectly to any environment.

A1-207.flavor_text: Its ability to evolve into many forms allows it to adapt smoothly and perfectly to any environment.

A1-208.flavor_text: Its ability to evolve into many forms allows it to adapt smoothly and perfectly to any environment.

ability.name: Ability: Data Scan
ability.effect: Once during your turn, you may look at the top card of your deck.

A1-209.flavor_text: State-of-the-art technology was used to create Porygon. It was the first artificial Pokémon to be created via computer programming.

attacks.name: Primal Wingbeat
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent shuffles their Active Pokémon back into their deck.

A1-210.flavor_text: This is a ferocious Pokémon from ancient times. Apparently even modern technology is incapable of producing a perfectly restored specimen.

A1-211.flavor_text: It is not satisfied unless it eats over 880 pounds of food every day. When it is done eating, it goes promptly to sleep.

A1-212.flavor_text: The way it brushes away grime with its tail can be helpful when cleaning. But its focus on spotlessness can make cleaning more of a hassle.

attacks.name: Do the Wave
attacks.effect: This attack does 30 damage for each of your Benched Pokémon.

A1-213.flavor_text: Its body secretes oil that this Pokémon spreads over its nest as a coating to protect it from dust. Cinccino won’t tolerate even a speck of the stuff.

A1-214.flavor_text: Its curly fleece is such an effective cushion that this Pokémon could fall off a cliff and stand right back up at the bottom, unharmed.

A1-215.flavor_text: Weave a carpet from its springy wool, and you end up with something closer to a trampoline. You’ll start to bounce the moment you set foot on it.

A1-227.flavor_text: There is a plant seed on its back right from the day this Pokémon is born. The seed slowly grows larger.

A1-228.flavor_text: Its pistils exude an incredibly foul odor. The horrid stench can cause fainting at a distance of 1.25 miles.

attacks.name: Double Horn
attacks.effect: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 50 damage for each heads.

A1-229.flavor_text: These Pokémon judge one another based on pincers. Thicker, more impressive pincers make for more popularity with the opposite gender.

attacks.name: Ember
attacks.effect: Discard a [R] Energy from this Pokémon.

A1-230.flavor_text: It has a preference for hot things. When it rains, steam is said to spout from the tip of its tail.

A1-231.flavor_text: This Pokémon can be seen galloping through fields at speeds of up to 150 mph, its fiery mane fluttering in the wind.

A1-232.flavor_text: When it retracts its long neck into its shell, it squirts out water with vigorous force.

attacks.name: Hyper Beam
attacks.effect: Discard a random Energy from your opponent's Active Pokémon.

A1-233.flavor_text: Once it appears, it goes on a rampage. It remains enraged until it demolishes everything around it.

attacks.name: Hydro Pump
attacks.effect: If this Pokémon has at least 3 extra [W] Energy attached, this attack does 70 more damage.

A1-234.flavor_text: A smart and kindhearted Pokémon, it glides across the surface of the sea while its beautiful song echoes around it.

A1-235.flavor_text: The more energy it charges up, the faster it gets. But this also makes it more likely to explode.

attacks.name: Psychic
attacks.effect: This attack does 30 more damage for each Energy attached to your opponent's Active Pokémon.

A1-236.flavor_text: It has an incredibly high level of intelligence. Some say that Alakazam remembers everything that ever happens to it, from birth till death.

A1-237.flavor_text: It is incredibly slow and dopey. It takes five seconds for it to feel pain when under attack.

A1-238.flavor_text: It lives about one yard underground, where it feeds on plant roots. It sometimes appears aboveground.

attacks.name: Growl
attacks.effect: During your opponent's next turn, attacks used by the Defending Pokémon do -20 damage.

A1-239.flavor_text: When the memory of its departed mother brings it to tears, its cries echo mournfully within the skull it wears on its head.

attacks.name: Lovestrike
attacks.effect: This attack does 50 more damage for each of your Benched Nidoking.

A1-240.flavor_text: Nidoqueen is better at defense than offense. With scales like armor, this Pokémon will shield its children from any kind of attack.

attacks.name: Poison Horn
attacks.effect: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Poisoned.

A1-241.flavor_text: When it goes on a rampage, it’s impossible to control. But in the presence of a Nidoqueen it’s lived with for a long time, Nidoking calms down.

A1-242.flavor_text: It loves to drink other creatures’ blood. It’s said that if it finds others of its kind going hungry, it sometimes shares the blood it’s gathered.

attacks.effect: Discard 2 [R] Energy from this Pokémon.

attacks.name: Circle Circuit
attacks.effect: This attack does 30 damage for each of your Benched [L] Pokémon.
