attacks.name: Growth Spurt
attacks.effect: Take a [G] Energy from your Energy Zone and attach it to this Pokémon.

A1a-1.flavor_text: Though it may look like it’s just a bunch of eggs, it’s a proper Pokémon. Exeggcute communicates with others of its kind via telepathy, apparently.

attacks.name: Psychic
attacks.effect: This attack does 20 more damage for each Energy attached to your opponent's Active Pokémon.

A1a-2.flavor_text: Each of Exeggutor’s three heads is thinking different thoughts. The three don’t seem to be very interested in one another.

attacks.name: Powerful Bloom
attacks.effect: Flip a coin for each Energy attached to this Pokémon. This attack does 50 damage for each heads.

A1a-4.flavor_text: Being exposed to sunlight makes its movements swifter. It uses vines more adeptly than its hands.

A1a-5.flavor_text: It moves along the ground as if sliding. Its swift movements befuddle its foes, and it then attacks with a vine whip.

ability.name: Ability: Jungle Totem
ability.effect: Each [G] Energy attached to your [G] Pokémon provides 2 [G] Energy. This effect doesn't stack.

A1a-6.flavor_text: It only gives its all against strong opponents who are not fazed by the glare from Serperior’s noble eyes.

A1a-7.flavor_text: Pokémon living in the forest eat the delicious caps on Morelull’s head. The caps regrow overnight.

attacks.name: Flickering Spores
attacks.effect: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Asleep.

A1a-8.flavor_text: Its flickering spores lure in prey and put them to sleep. Once this Pokémon has its prey snoozing, it drains their vitality with its fingertips.

attacks.name: Energy Whip
attacks.effect: If this Pokémon has at least 3 extra [G] Energy attached, this attack does 70 more damage.

A1a-9.flavor_text: After a piece of seaweed merged with debris from a sunken ship, it was reborn as this ghost Pokémon.

attacks.name: Stomp
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 more damage.

A1a-10.flavor_text: It can’t run properly when it’s newly born. As it races around with others of its kind, its legs grow stronger.

attacks.name: Rising Lunge
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 60 more damage.

A1a-11.flavor_text: This Pokémon can be seen galloping through fields at speeds of up to 150 mph, its fiery mane fluttering in the wind.

attacks.name: Fire Blast
attacks.effect: Discard 2 [R] Energy from this Pokémon.

A1a-12.flavor_text: Magmar dispatches its prey with fire. But it regrets this habit once it realizes that it has burned its intended prey to a charred crisp.

A1a-13.flavor_text: This Pokémon was called the Larva That Stole the Sun. The fire Larvesta spouts from its horns can cut right through a sheet of iron.

attacks.name: Volcanic Ash
attacks.effect: Discard 2 [R] Energy from this Pokémon. This attack does 80 damage to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon.

A1a-14.flavor_text: Its burning body causes it to be unpopular in hot parts of the world, but in cold ones, Volcarona is revered as an embodiment of the sun.

attacks.name: Venoshock
attacks.effect: If your opponent's Active Pokémon is Poisoned, this attack does 40 more damage.

A1a-15.flavor_text: It taunts its prey and lures them into narrow, rocky areas where it then sprays them with toxic gas to make them dizzy and take them down.

attacks.name: Poison Claws
attacks.effect: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Poisoned.

A1a-16.flavor_text: Salazzle makes its opponents light-headed with poisonous gas, then captivates them with alluring movements to turn them into loyal servants.

attacks.name: Leap Out
attacks.effect: Switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

A1a-17.flavor_text: An underpowered, pathetic Pokémon. It may jump high on rare occasions but never more than seven feet.

attacks.name: Rampaging Whirlpool
attacks.effect: Discard a random Energy from among the Energy attached to all Pokémon (both yours and your opponent's).

ability.name: Ability: Wash Out
ability.effect: As often as you like during your turn, you may move a [W] Energy from 1 of your Benched [W] Pokémon to your Active [W] Pokémon.

A1a-19.flavor_text: It lives close to water. Its long tail is ridged with a fin, which is often mistaken for a mermaid’s.

A1a-20.flavor_text: The line running down its side can store sunlight. It shines vividly at night.

attacks.name: Aqua Liner
attacks.effect: This attack does 50 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon.

A1a-21.flavor_text: With its shining light, it lures its prey close. However, the light also happens to attract ferocious fish Pokémon—its natural predators.

A1a-22.flavor_text: Its large front tooth is still growing in. When the tooth itches, this Pokémon will bite another Chewtle’s horn, and the two Pokémon will tussle.

attacks.name: Crunch
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, discard a random Energy from your opponent's Active Pokémon.

A1a-23.flavor_text: Its massive, jagged teeth can crush a boulder in a single bite. This Pokémon has an extremely vicious disposition.

attacks.name: Dive
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, during your opponent's next turn, prevent all damage from—and effects of—attacks done to this Pokémon.

A1a-24.flavor_text: It’s so strong that it can knock out some opponents in a single hit, but it also may forget what it’s battling midfight.

attacks.name: Circle Circuit
attacks.effect: This attack does 10 damage for each of your Benched [L] Pokémon.

A1a-25.flavor_text: When it is angered, it immediately discharges the energy stored in the pouches in its cheeks.

attacks.name: Gigashock
attacks.effect: This attack also does 20 damage to each of your opponent's Benched Pokémon.

A1a-26.flavor_text: Its tail discharges electricity into the ground, protecting it from getting shocked.

attacks.name: Thunder Spear
attacks.effect: This attack does 40 damage to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon.

A1a-27.flavor_text: Many power plants keep Ground-type Pokémon around as a defense against Electabuzz that come seeking electricity.

A1a-28.flavor_text: Joltik can be found clinging to other Pokémon. It’s soaking up static electricity because it can’t produce a charge on its own.

attacks.name: Electroweb
attacks.effect: During your opponent's next turn, the Defending Pokémon can't retreat.

A1a-29.flavor_text: It launches electrified fur from its abdomen as its means of attack. Opponents hit by the fur could be in for three full days and nights of paralysis.

attacks.name: Thunder Shock
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

A1a-30.flavor_text: It’s small and its electricity-generating organ is not fully developed, so it uses its tail to absorb electricity from people’s homes and charge itself.

attacks.name: Psy Report
attacks.effect: Your opponent reveals their hand.

A1a-31.flavor_text: Because it can use all kinds of moves, many scientists believe Mew to be the ancestor of Pokémon.

attacks.name: Genome Hacking
attacks.effect: Choose 1 of your opponent's Active Pokémon's attacks and use it as this attack.

attacks.name: Spike Draw
attacks.effect: Draw a card.

A1a-33.flavor_text: Psychic power allows these Pokémon to fly. Some say they were the guardians of an ancient city. Others say they were the guardians’ emissaries.

A1a-34.flavor_text: If this Pokémon stands near a TV, strange scenery will appear on the screen. That scenery is said to be from its home.

attacks.name: Mind Jack
attacks.effect: This attack does 20 more damage for each of your opponent's Benched Pokémon.

A1a-35.flavor_text: Whenever a Beheeyem visits a farm, a Dubwool mysteriously disappears.

attacks.name: Hypnotic Gaze
attacks.effect: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Asleep.

A1a-36.flavor_text: This Pokémon can draw forth the power hidden within blooming wild flowers. It is particularly fond of red flowers.

attacks.name: Leaf Drain
attacks.effect: Heal 10 damage from this Pokémon.

A1a-37.flavor_text: This Pokémon draws forth what power is left in withered flowers to make them healthy again. It holds a red flower.

attacks.name: Bloomshine
attacks.effect: Heal 20 damage from each of your Pokémon.

A1a-38.flavor_text: This Pokémon creates an impressive flower garden in its territory. It draws forth the power of the red flowers around its neck.

A1a-39.flavor_text: It eats its own weight in sugar every day. If it doesn’t get enough sugar, it becomes incredibly grumpy.

A1a-40.flavor_text: By taking in a person’s scent, it can sniff out their mental and physical condition. It’s hoped that this skill will have many medical applications.

attacks.name: Focus Fist
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.

A1a-41.flavor_text: It lives in groups in the treetops. If it loses sight of its group, it becomes infuriated by its loneliness.

A1a-42.flavor_text: It becomes wildly furious if it even senses someone looking at it. It chases anyone that meets its glare.

A1a-43.flavor_text: Geodude that have lived a long life have had all their edges smoothed out until they’re totally round. They also have a calm, quiet disposition.

A1a-44.flavor_text: It climbs up cliffs as it heads toward the peak of a mountain. As soon as it reaches the summit, it rolls back down the way it came.

attacks.name: Guard Press
attacks.effect: During your opponent's next turn, this Pokémon takes −30 damage from attacks.

A1a-45.flavor_text: When Golem grow old, they stop shedding their shells. Those that have lived a long, long time have shells green with moss.

ability.name: Ability: Primeval Law
ability.effect: Your opponent can't play any Pokémon from their hand to evolve their Active Pokémon.

attacks.name: Revenge
attacks.effect: If any of your Pokémon were Knocked Out by damage from an attack during your opponent's last turn, this attack does 60 more damage.

A1a-47.flavor_text: It slips into the shadows of others and mimics their powers and movements. As it improves, it becomes stronger than those it’s imitating.

A1a-48.flavor_text: The elemental composition of the rocks that form its body were found to match the bedrock of a land far away from this Pokémon’s habitat.

attacks.name: Division
attacks.effect: Put 1 random Koffing from your deck onto your Bench.

A1a-49.flavor_text: Its body is full of poisonous gas. It floats into garbage dumps, seeking out the fumes of raw, rotting trash.

attacks.name: Smokescreen
attacks.effect: During your opponent's next turn, if the Defending Pokémon tries to use an attack, your opponent flips a coin. If tails, that attack doesn't happen.

A1a-50.flavor_text: If one of the twin Koffing inflates, the other one deflates. It constantly mixes its poisonous gases.

A1a-51.flavor_text: It steals things from people just to amuse itself with their frustration. A rivalry exists between this Pokémon and Nickit.

A1a-52.flavor_text: Don’t be fooled by its gorgeous fur and elegant figure. This is a moody and vicious Pokémon.

A1a-53.flavor_text: Venipede and Sizzlipede are similar species, but when the two meet, a huge fight ensues.

attacks.name: Poison Sting
attacks.effect: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Poisoned.

A1a-54.flavor_text: This Pokémon spins itself rapidly and charges into its opponents. Its top speed is just over 60 mph.

attacks.name: Venoshock
attacks.effect: If your opponent's Active Pokémon is Poisoned, this attack does 50 more damage.

A1a-55.flavor_text: Scolipede latches on to its prey with the claws on its neck before slamming them into the ground and jabbing them with its claws’ toxic spikes.

ability.name: Ability: Rough Skin
ability.effect: If this Pokémon is in the Active Spot and is damaged by an attack from your opponent's Pokémon, do 20 damage to the Attacking Pokémon.

A1a-56.flavor_text: Druddigon lives in caves, but it never skips sunbathing—it won’t be able to move if its body gets too cold.

A1a-57.flavor_text: A common sight in forests and woods. It flaps its wings at ground level to kick up blinding sand.

A1a-58.flavor_text: The claws on its feet are well developed. It can carry prey such as an Exeggcute to its nest over 60 miles away.

attacks.name: Scattering Cyclone
attacks.effect: This attack does 20 more damage for each of your opponent's Benched Pokémon.

attacks.name: Fighting Tackle
attacks.effect: If your opponent's Active Pokémon is a Pokémon ex, this attack does 80 more damage.

A1a-60.flavor_text: When Tauros begins whipping itself with its tails, it’s a warning that the Pokémon is about to charge with astounding speed.

attacks.name: Continuous Steps
attacks.effect: Flip a coin until you get tails. This attack does 20 damage for each heads.

A1a-61.flavor_text: Its ability to evolve into many forms allows it to adapt smoothly and perfectly to any environment.

attacks.name: Mimic
attacks.effect: Shuffle your hand into your deck. Draw a card for each card in your opponent's hand.

A1a-62.flavor_text: It mimics the cries of other Pokémon to trick them into thinking it’s one of them. This way they won’t attack it.

attacks.name: Psychic
attacks.effect: This attack does 20 more damage for each Energy attached to your opponent's Active Pokémon.

A1a-69.flavor_text: Each of Exeggutor’s three heads is thinking different thoughts. The three don’t seem to be very interested in one another.

ability.name: Ability: Jungle Totem
ability.effect: Each [G] Energy attached to your [G] Pokémon provides 2 [G] Energy. This effect doesn't stack.

A1a-70.flavor_text: It only gives its all against strong opponents who are not fazed by the glare from Serperior’s noble eyes.

attacks.name: Venoshock
attacks.effect: If your opponent's Active Pokémon is Poisoned, this attack does 40 more damage.

A1a-71.flavor_text: It taunts its prey and lures them into narrow, rocky areas where it then sprays them with toxic gas to make them dizzy and take them down.

ability.name: Ability: Wash Out
ability.effect: As often as you like during your turn, you may move a [W] Energy from 1 of your Benched [W] Pokémon to your Active [W] Pokémon.

A1a-72.flavor_text: It lives close to water. Its long tail is ridged with a fin, which is often mistaken for a mermaid’s.

attacks.name: Thunder Shock
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

A1a-73.flavor_text: It’s small and its electricity-generating organ is not fully developed, so it uses its tail to absorb electricity from people’s homes and charge itself.

attacks.name: Revenge
attacks.effect: If any of your Pokémon were Knocked Out by damage from an attack during your opponent's last turn, this attack does 60 more damage.

A1a-74.flavor_text: It slips into the shadows of others and mimics their powers and movements. As it improves, it becomes stronger than those it’s imitating.

attacks.name: Powerful Bloom
attacks.effect: Flip a coin for each Energy attached to this Pokémon. This attack does 50 damage for each heads.

attacks.name: Rampaging Whirlpool
attacks.effect: Discard a random Energy from among the Energy attached to all Pokémon (both yours and your opponent's).

attacks.name: Genome Hacking
attacks.effect: Choose 1 of your opponent's Active Pokémon's attacks and use it as this attack.

ability.name: Ability: Primeval Law
ability.effect: Your opponent can't play any Pokémon from their hand to evolve their Active Pokémon.

attacks.name: Scattering Cyclone
attacks.effect: This attack does 20 more damage for each of your opponent's Benched Pokémon.

attacks.name: Genome Hacking
attacks.effect: Choose 1 of your opponent's Active Pokémon's attacks and use it as this attack.

ability.name: Ability: Primeval Law
ability.effect: Your opponent can't play any Pokémon from their hand to evolve their Active Pokémon.

attacks.name: Powerful Bloom
attacks.effect: Flip a coin for each Energy attached to this Pokémon. This attack does 50 damage for each heads.

attacks.name: Genome Hacking
attacks.effect: Choose 1 of your opponent's Active Pokémon's attacks and use it as this attack.

attacks.name: Blot
attacks.effect: Heal 10 damage from this Pokémon.

A2-1.flavor_text: If exposed to moonlight, it starts to move. It roams far and wide at night to scatter its seeds.

A2-2.flavor_text: Its pistils exude an incredibly foul odor. The horrid stench can cause fainting at a distance of 1.25 miles.

A2-3.flavor_text: Plentiful in the tropics. When it dances, its petals rub together and make a pleasant ringing sound.

A2-4.flavor_text: Hidden beneath a tangle of vines that grows nonstop even if the vines are torn off, this Pokémon’s true appearance remains a mystery.

attacks.name: Mega Drain
attacks.effect: Heal 30 damage from this Pokémon.

A2-5.flavor_text: Tangrowth has two arms that it can extend as it pleases. Recent research has shown that these arms are, in fact, bundles of vines.

A2-6.flavor_text: Its eyes can see 360 degrees without moving its head. It won’t miss prey—even those behind it.

attacks.name: Air Slash
attacks.effect: Discard a random Energy from this Pokémon.

A2-8.flavor_text: Its flowers give off a relaxing fragrance. The stronger its aroma, the healthier the Roselia is.

attacks.name: Poisonous Whip
attacks.effect: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Poisoned.

A2-9.flavor_text: After captivating opponents with its sweet scent, it lashes them with its thorny whips.

A2-10.flavor_text: It uses its whole body to photosynthesize when exposed to sunlight. Its shell is made from hardened soil.

A2-11.flavor_text: It lives along water in forests. In the daytime, it leaves the forest to sunbathe its treed shell.

attacks.name: Frenzy Plant
attacks.effect: During your next turn, this Pokémon can't use Frenzy Plant.

A2-12.flavor_text: Ancient people imagined that beneath the ground dwelt a gigantic Torterra.

A2-13.flavor_text: Its legs are short. Whenever it stumbles, its stiff antennae clack with a xylophone-like sound.

attacks.name: Entrancing Melody
attacks.effect: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Confused.

A2-14.flavor_text: By allowing its cry to resonate in the hollow of its belly, it produces a captivating sound.

A2-15.flavor_text: To shelter itself from cold, wintry winds, it covers itself with a cloak made of twigs and leaves.

attacks.name: Leaf Cutter
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 more damage.

A2-16.flavor_text: Its appearance changes depending on where it evolved. The materials on hand become a part of its body.

attacks.name: Call for Family
attacks.effect: Put 1 random Basic Pokémon from your deck onto your Bench.

A2-17.flavor_text: At night, Combee sleep in a group of about a hundred, packed closely together in a lump.

A2-18.flavor_text: It houses its colony in cells in its body and releases various pheromones to make those grubs do its bidding.

attacks.name: Flog
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 50 more damage.

A2-19.flavor_text: It attracts prey with its sweet-smelling saliva, then chomps down. It takes a whole day to eat prey.

attacks.name: Leafy Cyclone
attacks.effect: During your next turn, this Pokémon can't attack.

A2-20.flavor_text: When you see Leafeon asleep in a patch of sunshine, you’ll know it is using photosynthesis to produce clean air.

attacks.name: Energy Cutoff
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, discard a random Energy from your opponent's Active Pokémon.

A2-21.flavor_text: The lawn mower is one of the household appliances that led to the development of the Rotom Dex.

ability.name: Ability: Fragrant Flower Garden
ability.effect: Once during your turn, you may heal 10 damage from each of your Pokémon.

A2-22.flavor_text: It can dissolve toxins in the air to instantly transform ruined land into a lush field of flowers.

attacks.name: Stoke
attacks.effect: Take a [R] Energy from your Energy Zone and attach it to this Pokémon.

A2-23.flavor_text: Magmar dispatches its prey with fire. But it regrets this habit once it realizes that it has burned its intended prey to a charred crisp.

attacks.name: Bursting Inferno
attacks.effect: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Burned.

A2-24.flavor_text: When Magmortar inhales deeply, the fire burning in its belly intensifies, rising in temperature to over 3,600 degrees Fahrenheit.

A2-25.flavor_text: A common sight in volcanic areas, it slowly slithers around in a constant search for warm places.

attacks.name: Searing Flame
attacks.effect: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Burned.

A2-26.flavor_text: Its brittle shell occasionally spouts intense flames that circulate throughout its body.

A2-27.flavor_text: Its fiery rear end is fueled by gas made in its belly. Even rain can’t extinguish the fire.

A2-28.flavor_text: It skillfully controls the intensity of the fire on its tail to keep its foes at an ideal distance.

attacks.name: Flare Blitz
attacks.effect: Discard all [R] Energy from this Pokémon.

attacks.name: Heat Breath
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 more damage.

A2-30.flavor_text: If the convection microwave oven is not working properly, then the Rotom inhabiting it will become lethargic.

A2-31.flavor_text: It rubs its snout on the ground to find and dig up food. It sometimes discovers hot springs.

ability.name: Ability: Thick Fat
ability.effect: This Pokémon takes −20 damage from attacks from [R] or [W] Pokémon.

A2-32.flavor_text: If it charges at an enemy, the hairs on its back stand up straight. It is very sensitive to sound.

ability.name: Ability: Thick Fat
ability.effect: This Pokémon takes −30 damage from attacks from [R] or [W] Pokémon.

A2-33.flavor_text: This Pokémon can be spotted in wall paintings from as far back as 10,000 years ago. For a while, it was thought to have gone extinct.

ability.name: Ability: Crystal Body
ability.effect: Prevent all effects of attacks used by your opponent's Pokémon done to this Pokémon.

A2-34.flavor_text: With cold air that can reach temperatures as low as −328 degrees Fahrenheit, Regice instantly freezes any creature that approaches it.

attacks.name: Nap
attacks.effect: Heal 20 damage from this Pokémon.

A2-35.flavor_text: It doesn’t like to be taken care of. It’s difficult to bond with since it won’t listen to its Trainer.

A2-36.flavor_text: It lives alone, away from others. Apparently, every one of them believes it is the most important.

attacks.name: Aqua Jet
attacks.effect: This attack also does 30 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon.

A2-37.flavor_text: It swims as fast as a jet boat. The edges of its wings are sharp and can slice apart drifting ice.

A2-38.flavor_text: It spins its two tails like a screw to propel itself through water. The tails also slice clinging seaweed.

attacks.name: Jet Screw
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 more damage.

A2-39.flavor_text: With its flotation sac inflated, it can carry people on its back. It deflates the sac before it dives.

A2-40.flavor_text: It used to have a shell on its back long ago. This species is closely related to Pokémon like Shellder.

attacks.name: Muddy Water
attacks.effect: This attack also does 20 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon.

A2-41.flavor_text: They normally inhabit rocky seashores, but in times of continuous rain, they can sometimes be found in the mountains, far from the sea.

attacks.name: Elegant Swim
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, during your opponent's next turn, prevent all damage from—and effects of—attacks done to this Pokémon.

A2-42.flavor_text: The line running down its side can store sunlight. It shines vividly at night.

A2-43.flavor_text: With its shining light, it lures its prey close. However, the light also happens to attract ferocious fish Pokémon—its natural predators.

attacks.name: Ice Shard
attacks.effect: If your opponent's Active Pokémon is a [F] Pokémon, this attack does 30 more damage.

A2-44.flavor_text: During cold seasons, it migrates to the mountain’s lower reaches. It returns to the snow-covered summit in the spring.

A2-45.flavor_text: It lives a quiet life on mountains that are perpetually covered in snow. It hides itself by whipping up blizzards.

attacks.name: Ice Beam
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

A2-46.flavor_text: It can control its body temperature at will. This enables it to freeze the moisture in the atmosphere, creating flurries of diamond dust.

A2-47.flavor_text: This Rotom has entered a washing machine. It nods with satisfaction after it floods the surrounding area.

attacks.name: Blizzard
attacks.effect: This attack also does 10 damage to each of your opponent's Benched Pokémon.

A2-48.flavor_text: This Rotom has entered a refrigerator. It leaps around gleefully after it uses cold air to freeze the area around it.

attacks.name: Dimensional Storm
attacks.effect: Discard 3 [W] Energy from this Pokémon. This attack also does 20 damage to each of your opponent's Benched Pokémon.

attacks.name: Oceanic Gift
attacks.effect: Choose 2 of your Benched Pokémon. For each of those Pokémon, take a [W] Energy from your Energy Zone and attach it to that Pokémon.

A2-50.flavor_text: It is born with a wondrous power that lets it bond with any kind of Pokémon.

A2-51.flavor_text: The electromagnetic waves emitted by the units at the sides of its head expel antigravity, which allows it to float.

A2-52.flavor_text: Three Magnemite are linked by a strong magnetic force. Earaches will occur if you get too close.

attacks.name: Thunder Blast
attacks.effect: Discard a [L] Energy from this Pokémon.

A2-53.flavor_text: As it zooms through the sky, this Pokémon seems to be receiving signals of unknown origin while transmitting signals of unknown purpose.

attacks.name: Big Explosion
attacks.effect: This Pokémon also does 10 damage to itself.

A2-54.flavor_text: It rolls to move. If the ground is uneven, a sudden jolt from hitting a bump can cause it to explode.

A2-55.flavor_text: The more energy it charges up, the faster it gets. But this also makes it more likely to explode.

attacks.name: Charge
attacks.effect: Take a [L] Energy from your Energy Zone and attach it to this Pokémon.

A2-56.flavor_text: Many power plants keep Ground-type Pokémon around as a defense against Electabuzz that come seeking electricity.

attacks.name: Exciting Voltage
attacks.effect: If this Pokémon has at least 2 extra [L] Energy attached, this attack does 80 more damage.

A2-57.flavor_text: The amount of electrical energy this Pokémon produces is proportional to the rate of its pulse. The voltage jumps while Electivire is battling.

attacks.name: Hide
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, during your opponent's next turn, prevent all damage from—and effects of—attacks done to this Pokémon.

A2-58.flavor_text: The extension and contraction of its muscles generates electricity. It glows when in trouble.

A2-59.flavor_text: Strong electricity courses through the tips of its sharp claws. A light scratch causes fainting in foes.

attacks.name: Volt Bolt
attacks.effect: Discard all [L] Energy from this Pokémon. This attack does 120 damage to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon.

A2-60.flavor_text: It can see clearly through walls to track down its prey and seek its lost young.

attacks.name: Sparking Gadget
attacks.effect: If this Pokémon has a Pokémon Tool attached, this attack does 40 more damage.

attacks.name: Assault Laser
attacks.effect: If your opponent's Active Pokémon has a Pokémon Tool attached, this attack does 30 more damage.

A2-62.flavor_text: Its electricity-like body can enter some kinds of machines and take control in order to make mischief.

A2-63.flavor_text: The shell seems to be filled with joy. It is said that it will share good luck when treated kindly.

A2-64.flavor_text: They say that it will appear before kindhearted, caring people and shower them with happiness.

attacks.name: Overdrive Smash
attacks.effect: During your next turn, this Pokémon's Overdrive Smash attack does +60 damage.

A2-65.flavor_text: These Pokémon are never seen anywhere near conflict or turmoil. In recent times, they’ve hardly been seen at all.

A2-66.flavor_text: This Pokémon startles people in the middle of the night. It gathers fear as its energy.

attacks.name: Magical Delusion
attacks.effect: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Confused.

attacks.name: Teleport
attacks.effect: Switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

A2-68.flavor_text: The horns on its head provide a strong power that enables it to sense people’s emotions.

A2-69.flavor_text: It has a psychic power that enables it to distort the space around it and see into the future.

A2-70.flavor_text: If it finds bad children who won’t listen to their parents, it will spirit them away—or so it’s said.

A2-71.flavor_text: It seeks drifting will-o’-the-wisps and sucks them into its empty body. What happens inside is a mystery.

ability.name: Ability: Shadow Void
ability.effect: As often as you like during your turn, you may choose 1 of your Pokémon that has damage on it, and move all of its damage to this Pokémon.

A2-72.flavor_text: At the bidding of transmissions from the spirit world, it steals people and Pokémon away. No one knows whether it has a will of its own.

attacks.name: Expand
attacks.effect: During your opponent's next turn, this Pokémon takes −20 damage from attacks.

A2-73.flavor_text: It is whispered that any child who mistakes Drifloon for a balloon and holds on to it could wind up missing.

A2-74.flavor_text: Some say this Pokémon is a collection of souls burdened with regrets, silently drifting through the dusk.

attacks.name: Mind Boost
attacks.effect: Take a [P] Energy from your Energy Zone and attach it to Mesprit or Azelf.

A2-75.flavor_text: Known as “The Being of Knowledge.” It is said that it can wipe out the memory of those who see its eyes.

attacks.name: Supreme Blast
attacks.effect: You can use this attack only if you have Uxie and Azelf on your Bench. Discard all Energy from this Pokémon.

A2-76.flavor_text: Known as “The Being of Emotion.” It taught humans the nobility of sorrow, pain, and joy.

attacks.name: Psychic Arrow
attacks.effect: This attack does 20 damage to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon.

A2-77.flavor_text: Known as “The Being of Willpower.” It sleeps at the bottom of a lake to keep the world in balance.

ability.name: Ability: Levitate
ability.effect: If this Pokémon has any Energy attached, it has no Retreat Cost.

A2-78.flavor_text: This Pokémon is said to live in a world on the reverse side of ours, where common knowledge is distorted and strange.

attacks.name: Moonlight Gain
attacks.effect: Heal 20 damage from this Pokémon.

A2-79.flavor_text: Shiny particles are released from its wings like a veil. It is said to represent the crescent moon.

A2-80.flavor_text: Strong, but not too bright, this Pokémon can shatter even a skyscraper with its charging tackles.

A2-81.flavor_text: It begins walking on its hind legs after evolution. It can punch holes through boulders with its horn.

attacks.name: Mountain Swing
attacks.effect: Discard the top 3 cards of your deck.

A2-82.flavor_text: It can load up to three projectiles per arm into the holes in its hands. What launches out of those holes could be either rocks or Roggenrola.

A2-83.flavor_text: It usually clings to cliffs. When it spots its prey, it spreads its wings and glides down to attack.

attacks.name: Acrobatics
attacks.effect: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 20 more damage for each heads.

A2-84.flavor_text: It observes prey while hanging inverted from branches. When the chance presents itself, it swoops!

A2-85.flavor_text: It launches kicks while spinning. If it spins at high speed, it may bore its way into the ground.

A2-86.flavor_text: It moves less than an inch a year, but when it’s in a jam, it will spin and drill down into the ground in a split second.

ability.name: Ability: Exoskeleton
ability.effect: This Pokémon takes −20 damage from attacks.

A2-87.flavor_text: Every bit of Regirock’s body is made of stone. As parts of its body erode, this Pokémon sticks rocks to itself to repair what’s been lost.

A2-88.flavor_text: A primeval Pokémon, it possesses a hard and sturdy skull, lacking any intelligence within.

attacks.name: Head Smash
attacks.effect: If your opponent's Pokémon is Knocked Out by damage from this attack, this Pokémon also does 50 damage to itself.

A2-89.flavor_text: In ancient times, people would dig up fossils of this Pokémon and use its skull, which is harder than steel, to make helmets.

A2-90.flavor_text: Its appearance changes depending on where it evolved. The materials on hand become a part of its body.

A2-91.flavor_text: They communicate with one another using their auras. They are able to run all through the night.

ability.name: Ability: Fighting Coach
ability.effect: Attacks used by your [F] Pokémon do +20 damage to your opponent's Active Pokémon.

A2-92.flavor_text: It’s said that no foe can remain invisible to Lucario, since it can detect auras—even those of foes it could not otherwise see.

A2-93.flavor_text: It shuts its nostrils tight, then travels through sand as if walking. They form colonies of around 10.

A2-94.flavor_text: It is surprisingly quick to anger. It holds its mouth agape as a display of its strength.

attacks.name: Energized Blade
attacks.effect: This attack does 20 more damage for each Energy attached to your opponent's Active Pokémon.

A2-96.flavor_text: Feared and loathed by many, it is believed to bring misfortune to all those who see it at night.

attacks.name: Skill Dive
attacks.effect: This attack does 50 damage to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon.

A2-97.flavor_text: It is merciless by nature. It is said that it never forgives the mistakes of its Murkrow followers.

attacks.name: Double Scratch
attacks.effect: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 20 damage for each heads.

A2-98.flavor_text: This cunning Pokémon hides under the cover of darkness, waiting to attack its prey.

attacks.name: Scratching Nails
attacks.effect: If your opponent's Active Pokémon has damage on it, this attack does 40 more damage.

A2-100.flavor_text: A Pokémon with a persistent nature, it chases its chosen prey until the prey becomes exhausted.

A2-101.flavor_text: It will always obey the commands of a skilled Trainer. Its behavior arises from its living in packs in ancient times.

A2-102.flavor_text: It sprays a foul fluid from its rear. Its stench spreads over a mile radius, driving Pokémon away.

attacks.name: Poison Gas
attacks.effect: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Poisoned.

A2-103.flavor_text: It attacks by spraying a horribly smelly fluid from the tip of its tail. Attacks from above confound it.

attacks.name: Swirling Disaster
attacks.effect: This attack does 10 damage to each of your opponent's Pokémon.

A2-104.flavor_text: Its constant mischief and misdeeds resulted in it being bound to an Odd Keystone by a mysterious spell.

A2-105.flavor_text: After burrowing into the sand, it waits patiently for prey to come near. This Pokémon and Sizzlipede share common descent.

attacks.name: Cross Poison
attacks.effect: Flip 4 coins. This attack does 40 damage for each heads. If at least 2 of them are heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Poisoned.

A2-106.flavor_text: Its poison is potent, but it rarely sees use. This Pokémon prefers to use physical force instead, going on rampages with its car-crushing strength.

attacks.name: Group Beatdown
attacks.effect: Flip a coin for each Pokémon you have in play. This attack does 20 damage for each heads.

A2-107.flavor_text: Inflating its poison sacs, it fills the area with an odd sound and hits flinching opponents with a poison jab.

attacks.name: Group Beatdown
attacks.effect: Flip a coin for each Pokémon you have in play. This attack does 40 damage for each heads.

A2-108.flavor_text: Swaying and dodging the attacks of its foes, it weaves its flexible body in close, then lunges out with its poisonous claws.

attacks.name: Dark Void
attacks.effect: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Asleep.

A2-109.flavor_text: It chases people and Pokémon from its territory by causing them to experience deep, nightmarish slumbers.

ability.name: Ability: Nightmare Aura
ability.effect: Whenever you attach a [D] Energy from your Energy Zone to this Pokémon, do 20 damage to your opponent's Active Pokémon.

attacks.name: Metal Arms
attacks.effect: If this Pokémon has a Pokémon Tool attached, this attack does 30 more damage.

A2-111.flavor_text: People fashion swords from Skarmory’s shed feathers, so this Pokémon is a popular element in heraldic designs.

A2-112.flavor_text: Registeel’s body is made of a strange material that is flexible enough to stretch and shrink but also more durable than any metal.

A2-113.flavor_text: A mild-mannered, herbivorous Pokémon, it used its face to dig up tree roots to eat. The skin on its face was plenty tough.

ability.name: Ability: Guarded Grill
ability.effect: If any damage is done to this Pokémon by attacks, flip a coin. If heads, this Pokémon takes −100 damage from that attack.

A2-114.flavor_text: The bones of its face are huge and hard, so they were mistaken for its spine until after this Pokémon was successfully restored.

attacks.name: Iron Head
attacks.effect: Flip a coin until you get tails. This attack does 30 more damage for each heads.

A2-115.flavor_text: Its appearance changes depending on where it evolved. The materials on hand become a part of its body.

A2-116.flavor_text: Ancient people believed that the pattern on Bronzor’s back contained a mysterious power.

attacks.name: Guard Press
attacks.effect: During your opponent's next turn, this Pokémon takes −20 damage from attacks.

A2-117.flavor_text: In ages past, this Pokémon was revered as a bringer of rain. It was found buried in the ground.

attacks.name: Triple Nose
attacks.effect: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 50 more damage for each heads.

A2-118.flavor_text: Although it can control its units known as Mini-Noses, they sometimes get lost and don’t come back.

attacks.name: Metallic Turbo
attacks.effect: Take 2 [M] Energy from your Energy Zone and attach it to 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

attacks.name: Steel Tackle
attacks.effect: This Pokémon also does 20 damage to itself.

A2-120.flavor_text: It dwells in volcanic caves. It digs in with its cross-shaped feet to crawl on ceilings and walls.

A2-121.flavor_text: It skulks in caves, and when prey or an enemy passes by, it leaps out and chomps them. The force of its attack sometimes chips its teeth.

A2-122.flavor_text: In rare cases, it molts and sheds its scales. Medicine containing its scales as an ingredient will make a weary body feel invigorated.

ability.name: Ability: Reckless Shearing
ability.effect: You must discard a card from your hand in order to use this Ability. Once during your turn, you may draw a card.

A2-123.flavor_text: It is said that when one runs at high speed, its wings create blades of wind that can fell nearby trees.

A2-124.flavor_text: If this Pokémon’s sticky saliva gets on you and you don’t clean it off, an intense itch will set in. The itch won’t go away, either.

attacks.name: Licking Fury
attacks.effect: Flip a coin until you get tails. This attack does 40 more damage for each heads.

attacks.name: Quick Attack
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 more damage.

A2-126.flavor_text: Its ability to evolve into many forms allows it to adapt smoothly and perfectly to any environment.

A2-127.flavor_text: State-of-the-art technology was used to create Porygon. It was the first artificial Pokémon to be created via computer programming.

A2-128.flavor_text: This is a Porygon that was updated with special data. Porygon2 develops itself by learning about many different subjects all on its own.

attacks.name: Buggy Beam
attacks.effect: Change the type of the next Energy that will be generated for your opponent to 1 of the following at random: [G],[R],[W],[L],[P],[F],[D], or [M].

A2-129.flavor_text: Porygon-Z had a program installed to allow it to move between dimensions, but the program also caused instability in Porygon-Z’s behavior.

A2-130.flavor_text: As it did more and more with its tail, its hands became clumsy. It makes its nest high in the treetops.

attacks.name: Double Hit
attacks.effect: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 40 damage for each heads.

A2-131.flavor_text: It uses its tails for everything. If it wraps both of its tails around you and gives you a squeeze, that’s proof it really likes you.

attacks.name: Pluck
attacks.effect: Before doing damage, discard all Pokémon Tools from your opponent's Active Pokémon.

A2-132.flavor_text: They flock around mountains and fields, chasing after bug Pokémon. Their singing is noisy and annoying.

A2-133.flavor_text: Recognizing their own weakness, they always live in a group. When alone, a Staravia cries noisily.

attacks.name: Clutch
attacks.effect: During your opponent's next turn, the Defending Pokémon can't retreat.

A2-134.flavor_text: When Staravia evolve into Staraptor, they leave the flock to live alone. They have sturdy wings.

attacks.name: Super Fang
attacks.effect: Halve your opponent's Active Pokémon's remaining HP, rounded down.

A2-135.flavor_text: With nerves of steel, nothing can perturb it. It is more agile and active than it appears.

A2-136.flavor_text: It busily makes its nest with stacks of branches and roots it has cut up with its sharp incisors.

A2-137.flavor_text: If both of Buneary’s ears are rolled up, something is wrong with its body or mind. It’s a sure sign the Pokémon is in need of care.

attacks.name: Jump Kick
attacks.effect: This attack also does 20 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon.

A2-138.flavor_text: Lopunny is constantly monitoring its surroundings. If danger approaches, this Pokémon responds with superdestructive kicks.

attacks.name: Pose
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.

A2-139.flavor_text: It claws if displeased and purrs when affectionate. Its fickleness is very popular among some.

attacks.name: Interrupt
attacks.effect: Your opponent reveals their hand. Choose a card you find there and shuffle it into your opponent's deck.

A2-140.flavor_text: It would claim another Pokémon’s nest as its own if it finds a nest sufficiently comfortable.

attacks.name: Fury Attack
attacks.effect: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 20 damage for each heads.

A2-141.flavor_text: It mimics the cries of other Pokémon to trick them into thinking it’s one of them. This way they won’t attack it.

attacks.name: Spin Storm
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, put your opponent's Active Pokémon into their hand.

A2-142.flavor_text: This Rotom has entered an electric fan. It smirks with satisfaction over a prank well pulled after it blows away everything around it.

attacks.name: Raging Hammer
attacks.effect: This attack does more damage equal to the damage this Pokémon has on it.

A2-143.flavor_text: It is said to have made Pokémon that look like itself from a special ice mountain, rocks, and magma.

attacks.name: Mega Drain
attacks.effect: Heal 30 damage from this Pokémon.

A2-156.flavor_text: Tangrowth has two arms that it can extend as it pleases. Recent research has shown that these arms are, in fact, bundles of vines.

attacks.name: Call for Family
attacks.effect: Put 1 random Basic Pokémon from your deck onto your Bench.

A2-157.flavor_text: At night, Combee sleep in a group of about a hundred, packed closely together in a lump.

attacks.name: Flog
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 50 more damage.

A2-158.flavor_text: It attracts prey with its sweet-smelling saliva, then chomps down. It takes a whole day to eat prey.

ability.name: Ability: Fragrant Flower Garden
ability.effect: Once during your turn, you may heal 10 damage from each of your Pokémon.

A2-159.flavor_text: It can dissolve toxins in the air to instantly transform ruined land into a lush field of flowers.

ability.name: Ability: Thick Fat
ability.effect: This Pokémon takes −30 damage from attacks from [R] or [W] Pokémon.

A2-160.flavor_text: This Pokémon can be spotted in wall paintings from as far back as 10,000 years ago. For a while, it was thought to have gone extinct.

attacks.name: Muddy Water
attacks.effect: This attack also does 20 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon.

A2-161.flavor_text: They normally inhabit rocky seashores, but in times of continuous rain, they can sometimes be found in the mountains, far from the sea.

attacks.name: Oceanic Gift
attacks.effect: Choose 2 of your Benched Pokémon. For each of those Pokémon, take a [W] Energy from your Energy Zone and attach it to that Pokémon.

A2-162.flavor_text: It is born with a wondrous power that lets it bond with any kind of Pokémon.

attacks.name: Hide
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, during your opponent's next turn, prevent all damage from—and effects of—attacks done to this Pokémon.

A2-163.flavor_text: The extension and contraction of its muscles generates electricity. It glows when in trouble.

attacks.name: Assault Laser
attacks.effect: If your opponent's Active Pokémon has a Pokémon Tool attached, this attack does 30 more damage.

A2-164.flavor_text: Its electricity-like body can enter some kinds of machines and take control in order to make mischief.

attacks.name: Expand
attacks.effect: During your opponent's next turn, this Pokémon takes −20 damage from attacks.

A2-165.flavor_text: It is whispered that any child who mistakes Drifloon for a balloon and holds on to it could wind up missing.

attacks.name: Supreme Blast
attacks.effect: You can use this attack only if you have Uxie and Azelf on your Bench. Discard all Energy from this Pokémon.

A2-166.flavor_text: Known as “The Being of Emotion.” It taught humans the nobility of sorrow, pain, and joy.

ability.name: Ability: Levitate
ability.effect: If this Pokémon has any Energy attached, it has no Retreat Cost.

A2-167.flavor_text: This Pokémon is said to live in a world on the reverse side of ours, where common knowledge is distorted and strange.

attacks.name: Moonlight Gain
attacks.effect: Heal 20 damage from this Pokémon.

A2-168.flavor_text: Shiny particles are released from its wings like a veil. It is said to represent the crescent moon.

attacks.name: Mountain Swing
attacks.effect: Discard the top 3 cards of your deck.

A2-169.flavor_text: It can load up to three projectiles per arm into the holes in its hands. What launches out of those holes could be either rocks or Roggenrola.

ability.name: Ability: Fighting Coach
ability.effect: Attacks used by your [F] Pokémon do +20 damage to your opponent's Active Pokémon.

A2-170.flavor_text: It’s said that no foe can remain invisible to Lucario, since it can detect auras—even those of foes it could not otherwise see.

A2-171.flavor_text: It shuts its nostrils tight, then travels through sand as if walking. They form colonies of around 10.

attacks.name: Swirling Disaster
attacks.effect: This attack does 10 damage to each of your opponent's Pokémon.

A2-172.flavor_text: Its constant mischief and misdeeds resulted in it being bound to an Odd Keystone by a mysterious spell.

attacks.name: Group Beatdown
attacks.effect: Flip a coin for each Pokémon you have in play. This attack does 20 damage for each heads.

A2-173.flavor_text: Inflating its poison sacs, it fills the area with an odd sound and hits flinching opponents with a poison jab.

attacks.name: Steel Tackle
attacks.effect: This Pokémon also does 20 damage to itself.

A2-174.flavor_text: It dwells in volcanic caves. It digs in with its cross-shaped feet to crawl on ceilings and walls.

ability.name: Ability: Reckless Shearing
ability.effect: You must discard a card from your hand in order to use this Ability. Once during your turn, you may draw a card.

A2-175.flavor_text: It is said that when one runs at high speed, its wings create blades of wind that can fell nearby trees.

A2a-1.flavor_text: It loves sweet nectar. To keep all the nectar to itself, it hurls rivals away with its prized horn.

A2a-2.flavor_text: To shelter itself from cold, wintry winds, it covers itself with a cloak made of twigs and leaves.

A2a-3.flavor_text: It loves the honey of flowers and steals honey collected by Combee.

A2a-4.flavor_text: At night, Combee sleep in a group of about a hundred, packed closely together in a lump.

attacks.name: Reckless Charge
attacks.effect: This Pokémon also does 30 damage to itself.

A2a-5.flavor_text: It houses its colony in cells in its body and releases various pheromones to make those grubs do its bidding.

A2a-6.flavor_text: It nimbly dashes about to avoid getting pecked by bird Pokémon that would love to make off with its small, nutrient-rich storage ball.

attacks.name: Worry Seed
attacks.effect: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Confused.

A2a-7.flavor_text: As a bud, it barely moves. It sits still, placidly waiting for sunlight to appear.

attacks.name: Leech Seed
attacks.effect: Heal 20 damage from this Pokémon.

A2a-8.flavor_text: After absorbing plenty of sunlight, Cherrim takes this form. It’s full of energy while it’s like this, and its liveliness will go on until sundown.

ability.name: Ability: Power Link

ability.effect: Once during your turn, if this Pokémon is in the Active Spot, you may take a [G] Energy from your Energy Zone and attach it to 1 of your [G] Pokémon.

A2a-11.flavor_text: It is smart enough to hunt in packs. It uses a variety of cries for communicating with others.

attacks.name: Corner
attacks.effect: During your opponent's next turn, the Defending Pokémon can't retreat.

A2a-12.flavor_text: If you are burned by the flames it shoots from its mouth, the pain will never go away.

attacks.name: Ragin’ Mad Strike
attacks.effect: If this Pokémon has damage on it, this attack does 40 more damage.

ability.name: Ability: Speed Link
ability.effect: If you have Arceus or Arceus ex in play, this Pokémon has no Retreat Cost.

A2a-13.flavor_text: It dwells in volcanic caves. It digs in with its cross-shaped feet to crawl on ceilings and walls.

A2a-14.flavor_text: The fur on its body naturally repels water. It can stay dry even when it plays in the water.

A2a-15.flavor_text: Its long ears are superb sensors. It can distinguish the movements of things in water and tell what they are.

A2a-16.flavor_text: Its two whiskers provide a sensitive radar. Even in muddy waters, it can detect its prey’s location.

attacks.name: Thrash
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 60 more damage. If tails, this Pokémon also does 20 damage to itself.

A2a-17.flavor_text: It is extremely protective of its territory. If any foe approaches, it attacks using vicious tremors.

A2a-18.flavor_text: It is said that several Snorunt gather under giant leaves and live together in harmony.

attacks.name: Blizzard
attacks.effect: This attack also does 10 damage to each of your opponent's Benched Pokémon.

A2a-19.flavor_text: When it finds humans or Pokémon it likes, it freezes them and takes them to its chilly den, where they become decorations.

A2a-20.flavor_text: During cold seasons, it migrates to the mountain’s lower reaches. It returns to the snow-covered summit in the spring.

ability.name: Ability: Vigor Link
ability.effect: If you have Arceus or Arceus ex in play, attacks used by this Pokémon cost 1 less [C] Energy.

A2a-21.flavor_text: It lives a quiet life on mountains that are perpetually covered in snow. It hides itself by whipping up blizzards.

ability.name: Ability: Snowy Terrain
ability.effect: During Pokémon Checkup, if this Pokémon is in the Active Spot, do 10 damage to your opponent's Active Pokémon.

attacks.name: Zone Smash
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 60 more damage.

A2a-23.flavor_text: It soars across the sky in a form that greatly resembles the creator of all things. Perhaps this imitation of appearance is Palkia’s strategy for gaining Arceus’s powers.

attacks.name: Water Pulse
attacks.effect: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Asleep.

A2a-24.flavor_text: When the water warms, they inflate the flotation sac on their heads and drift languidly on the sea in packs.

attacks.name: Spark
attacks.effect: This attack also does 10 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon.

A2a-25.flavor_text: When it is angered, it immediately discharges the energy stored in the pouches in its cheeks.

attacks.name: Spark
attacks.effect: This attack also does 20 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon.

ability.name: Ability: Resilience Link
ability.effect: If you have Arceus or Arceus ex in play, this Pokémon takes −30 damage from attacks.

A2a-26.flavor_text: Its tail discharges electricity into the ground, protecting it from getting shocked.

A2a-27.flavor_text: It stores static electricity in its fur for discharging. It gives off sparks if a storm approaches.

attacks.name: Flash
attacks.effect: During your opponent's next turn, if the Defending Pokémon tries to use an attack, your opponent flips a coin. If tails, that attack doesn't happen.

A2a-28.flavor_text: It stimulates its own muscles with electricity, so it can move quickly. It eases its soreness with electricity, too, so it can recover quickly as well.

A2a-29.flavor_text: It is said that happiness will come to those who see a gathering of Clefairy dancing under a full moon.

attacks.name: Moonblast
attacks.effect: During your opponent's next turn, attacks used by the Defending Pokémon do −30 damage.

A2a-30.flavor_text: A timid fairy Pokémon that is rarely seen, it will run and hide the moment it senses people.

attacks.name: Astonish
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent reveals a random card from their hand and shuffles it into their deck.

A2a-31.flavor_text: It wraps its opponent in its gas-like body, slowly weakening its prey by poisoning it through the skin.

A2a-32.flavor_text: It likes to lurk in the dark and tap shoulders with a gaseous hand. Its touch causes endless shuddering.

attacks.name: Hypnoblast
attacks.effect: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Asleep.

A2a-33.flavor_text: To steal the life of its target, it slips into the prey’s shadow and silently waits for an opportunity.

ability.name: Ability: CHECK
ability.effect: Once during your turn, you may choose either player. Look at the top card of that player's deck.

A2a-34.flavor_text: Its flat, thin body is always stuck on walls. Its shape appears to have some meaning.

attacks.effect: This attack does 20 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon.

ability.name: Ability: Speed Link
ability.effect: If you have Arceus or Arceus ex in play, this Pokémon has no Retreat Cost.

A2a-35.flavor_text: Its electricity-like body can enter some kinds of machines and take control in order to make mischief.

attacks.name: Fighting Headbutt
attacks.effect: If your opponent's Active Pokémon is a Pokémon ex, this attack does 30 more damage.

A2a-36.flavor_text: Although it always pretends to be a tree, its composition appears more similar to rock than to vegetation.

A2a-37.flavor_text: This Pokémon lives and nests on a riverbank. After playing in the mud, it won’t be able to settle down unless it washes its body.

attacks.name: Rolling Spin
attacks.effect: During your next turn, this Pokémon's Rolling Spin attack does +60 damage.

A2a-38.flavor_text: Donphan is covered in tough hide, so even being hit by a car won’t faze this Pokémon. However, it is extremely susceptible to rain.

A2a-39.flavor_text: Born deep underground, this Pokémon becomes a pupa after eating enough dirt to make a mountain.

A2a-40.flavor_text: Its shell is as hard as bedrock, and it is also very strong. Its thrashing can topple a mountain.

ability.name: Ability: Power Link
ability.effect: If you have Arceus or Arceus ex in play, attacks used by this Pokémon do +30 damage to your opponent's Active Pokémon.

A2a-41.flavor_text: Extremely strong, it can change the landscape. It is so insolent that it doesn’t care about others.

A2a-42.flavor_text: It moves less than an inch a year, but when it’s in a jam, it will spin and drill down into the ground in a split second.

A2a-43.flavor_text: It never skips its daily yoga training. It heightens its inner strength through meditation.

attacks.name: Kick Shot
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.

A2a-44.flavor_text: Through yoga training, it gained the psychic power to predict its foe’s next move.

A2a-45.flavor_text: It skulks in caves, and when prey or an enemy passes by, it leaps out and chomps them. The force of its attack sometimes chips its teeth.

A2a-46.flavor_text: In rare cases, it molts and sheds its scales. Medicine containing its scales as an ingredient will make a weary body feel invigorated.

attacks.name: Linear Attack
attacks.effect: This attack does 50 damage to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon.

A2a-48.flavor_text: It emits ultrasonic waves from its mouth to check its surroundings. Even in tight caves, Zubat flies around with skill.

A2a-49.flavor_text: It loves to drink other creatures’ blood. It’s said that if it finds others of its kind going hungry, it sometimes shares the blood it’s gathered.

ability.name: Ability: Cunning Link
ability.effect: Once during your turn, if you have Arceus or Arceus ex in play, you may do 30 damage to your opponent's Active Pokémon.

A2a-50.flavor_text: Both of its legs have turned into wings. Without a sound, Crobat flies swiftly toward its prey and sinks its fangs into the nape of its target’s neck.

A2a-51.flavor_text: Inflating its poison sacs, it fills the area with an odd sound and hits flinching opponents with a poison jab.

attacks.name: Toxic
attacks.effect: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Poisoned. Do 20 damage to this Pokémon instead of the usual amount for this Special Condition.

A2a-53.flavor_text: The electromagnetic waves emitted by the units at the sides of its head expel antigravity, which allows it to float.

A2a-54.flavor_text: Three Magnemite are linked by a strong magnetic force. Earaches will occur if you get too close.

ability.name: Ability: Resilience Link
ability.effect: If you have Arceus or Arceus ex in play, this Pokémon takes −30 damage from attacks.

attacks.name: Iron Beam Breaker
attacks.effect: If your opponent's Active Pokémon is a [M] Pokémon, this attack does 30 more damage.

attacks.name: Defensive Unit

A2a-62.flavor_text: Its ability to evolve into many forms allows it to adapt smoothly and perfectly to any environment.

attacks.name: Collapse

attacks.name: Silent Wing

attacks.name: Ultimate Force
attacks.effect: This attack does 20 more damage for each of your Benched Pokémon.

ability.name: Ability: Fabled Luster
ability.effect: This Pokémon can't be affected by any Special Conditions.

attacks.name: Ultimate Force
attacks.effect: This attack does 20 more damage for each of your Benched Pokémon.

ability.name: Ability: Fabled Luster
ability.effect: This Pokémon can't be affected by any Special Conditions.

A2b-1.flavor_text: Often found in forests and grasslands. It has a sharp, toxic barb of around two inches on top of its head.

attacks.name: String Shot
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

A2b-2.flavor_text: Almost incapable of moving, this Pokémon can only harden its shell to protect itself when it is in danger.

attacks.name: Crushing Spear
attacks.effect: Discard a random Energy from your opponent's Active Pokémon.

attacks.name: Guillotine Rush
attacks.effect: Flip a coin until you get tails. This attack does 40 more damage for each heads.

A2b-4.flavor_text: These Pokémon judge one another based on pincers. Thicker, more impressive pincers make for more popularity with the opposite gender.

attacks.name: Cry for Help
attacks.effect: Put 1 random [G] Pokémon from your deck into your hand.

A2b-5.flavor_text: Its fluffy fur is similar in composition to plants. This Pokémon frequently washes its face to keep it from drying out.

A2b-6.flavor_text: Floragato deftly wields the vine hidden beneath its long fur, slamming the hard flower bud against its opponents.

attacks.name: Fighting Claws
attacks.effect: If your opponent's Active Pokémon is a Pokémon ex, this attack does 70 more damage.

A2b-7.flavor_text: This Pokémon uses the reflective fur lining its cape to camouflage the stem of its flower, creating the illusion that the flower is floating.

A2b-8.flavor_text: It has a preference for hot things. When it rains, steam is said to spout from the tip of its tail.

A2b-9.flavor_text: It has a barbaric nature. In battle, it whips its fiery tail around and slashes away with sharp claws.

attacks.name: Stoke
attacks.effect: Take 3 [R] Energy from your Energy Zone and attach it to this Pokémon.

A2b-11.flavor_text: Magmar dispatches its prey with fire. But it regrets this habit once it realizes that it has burned its intended prey to a charred crisp.

attacks.name: Smoke Bomb
attacks.effect: During your opponent's next turn, if the Defending Pokémon tries to use an attack, your opponent flips a coin. If tails, that attack doesn't happen.

A2b-12.flavor_text: When Magmortar inhales deeply, the fire burning in its belly intensifies, rising in temperature to over 3,600 degrees Fahrenheit.

attacks.name: Blaze Tackle
attacks.effect: This Pokémon also does 10 damage to itself.

A2b-13.flavor_text: When heated by fire energy, its horns can get hotter than 1,800 degrees Fahrenheit. Those gored by them will suffer both wounds and burns.

A2b-14.flavor_text: Tentacool is not a particularly strong swimmer. It drifts across the surface of shallow seas as it searches for prey.

A2b-15.flavor_text: When the red orbs on Tentacruel’s head glow brightly, watch out. The Pokémon is about to fire off a burst of ultrasonic waves.

A2b-16.flavor_text: It inflates its flotation sac, keeping its face above water in order to watch for prey movement.

attacks.name: Water Arrow
attacks.effect: This attack does 30 damage to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon.

A2b-17.flavor_text: With its flotation sac inflated, it can carry people on its back. It deflates the sac before it dives.

attacks.name: Spring Out
attacks.effect: 1 of your opponent's Pokémon is chosen at random. Do 30 damage to it.

A2b-18.flavor_text: This Pokémon can pick up the scent of a Veluza just over 65 feet away and will hide itself in the sand.

attacks.name: Pop Out Throughout
attacks.effect: 1 of your opponent's Pokémon is chosen at random 3 times. For each time a Pokémon was chosen, do 50 damage to it.

attacks.name: Ocean Cyclone
attacks.effect: This attack also does 10 damage to each of your opponent's Benched Pokémon.

A2b-20.flavor_text: This Pokémon is a glutton, but it’s bad at getting food. It teams up with a Tatsugiri to catch prey.

ability.name: Ability: Retreat Directive
ability.effect: Your Active Dondozo has no Retreat Cost.

A2b-21.flavor_text: This is a small dragon Pokémon. It lives inside the mouth of Dondozo to protect itself from enemies on the outside.

attacks.name: Thunderbolt
attacks.effect: Discard all Energy from this Pokémon.

A2b-23.flavor_text: It rolls to move. If the ground is uneven, a sudden jolt from hitting a bump can cause it to explode.

attacks.name: Tumbling Attack
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 more damage.

A2b-24.flavor_text: The more energy it charges up, the faster it gets. But this also makes it more likely to explode.

attacks.name: Plasma
attacks.effect: Take a [L] Energy from your Energy Zone and attach it to 1 of your Benched [L] Pokémon.

A2b-25.flavor_text: It’s one of the kinds of Pokémon with electric cheek pouches. It shoots charges from its tail.

A2b-26.flavor_text: It has underdeveloped electric sacs on its cheeks. These sacs can produce electricity only if Pawmi rubs them furiously with the pads on its forepaws.

A2b-27.flavor_text: When its group is attacked, Pawmo is the first to leap into battle, defeating enemies with a fighting technique that utilizes electric shocks.

ability.name: Ability: Counterattack
ability.effect: If this Pokémon is in the Active Spot and is damaged by an attack from your opponent's Pokémon, do 20 damage to the Attacking Pokémon.

A2b-28.flavor_text: This Pokémon normally is slow to react, but once it enters battle, it will strike down its enemies with lightning-fast movements.

A2b-29.flavor_text: This Pokémon uses its psychic powers while it sleeps. The contents of Abra’s dreams affect the powers that the Pokémon wields.

A2b-30.flavor_text: Using its psychic power, Kadabra levitates as it sleeps. It uses its springy tail as a pillow.

attacks.name: Psychic Suppression
attacks.effect: This attack also does 20 damage to each of your opponent's Benched Pokémon that has any Energy attached.

A2b-31.flavor_text: It has an incredibly high level of intelligence. Some say that Alakazam remembers everything that ever happens to it, from birth till death.

attacks.name: Juggling
attacks.effect: Flip 4 coins. This attack does 20 damage for each heads.

A2b-32.flavor_text: The broadness of its hands may be no coincidence—many scientists believe its palms became enlarged specifically for pantomiming.

A2b-33.flavor_text: It is whispered that any child who mistakes Drifloon for a balloon and holds on to it could wind up missing.

A2b-34.flavor_text: It can generate and release gas within its body. That’s how it can control the altitude of its drift.

attacks.name: Chaotic Impact
attacks.effect: This Pokémon also does 20 damage to itself.

ability.name: Ability: Broken-Space Bellow
ability.effect: Once during your turn, you may take a [P] Energy from your Energy Zone and attach it to this Pokémon. If you use this Ability, your turn ends.

attacks.name: Chest-ouflage
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, during your opponent's next turn, prevent all damage from—and effects of—attacks done to this Pokémon.

A2b-36.flavor_text: This Pokémon was born inside a treasure chest about 1,500 years ago. It sucks the life-force out of scoundrels who try to steal the treasure.

A2b-37.flavor_text: Its whole body is composed of muscles. Even though it’s the size of a human child, it can hurl 100 grown-ups.

attacks.name: Pummel
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 more damage.

A2b-38.flavor_text: Its muscular body is so powerful, it must wear a power-save belt to be able to regulate its motions.

attacks.name: Power Press
attacks.effect: If this Pokémon has at least 2 extra [F] Energy attached, this attack does 50 more damage.

A2b-39.flavor_text: It quickly swings its four arms to rock its opponents with ceaseless punches and chops from all angles.

A2b-40.flavor_text: This amazing Pokémon has an awesome sense of balance. It can kick in succession from any position.

A2b-41.flavor_text: Its punches slice the air. They are launched at such high speed, even a slight graze could cause a burn.

A2b-42.flavor_text: They communicate with one another using their auras. They are able to run all through the night.

attacks.name: Aura Sphere
attacks.effect: This attack also does 30 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon.

attacks.name: Double Kick
attacks.effect: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 50 damage for each heads.

A2b-44.flavor_text: This Pokémon apparently ties the base of its neck into a knot so that energy stored in its belly does not escape from its beak.

A2b-45.flavor_text: By dislocating its jaw, it can swallow prey larger than itself. After a meal, it curls up and rests.

attacks.name: Venomous Fang
attacks.effect: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Poisoned.

A2b-46.flavor_text: After stunning its opponents with the pattern on its stomach, it quickly wraps them up in its body and waits for them to stop moving.

attacks.name: Poison Jab
attacks.effect: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Poisoned.

A2b-47.flavor_text: After losing a territorial struggle, Wooper began living on land. The Pokémon changed over time, developing a poisonous film to protect its body.

attacks.name: Venoshock
attacks.effect: If your opponent's Active Pokémon is Poisoned, this attack does 60 more damage.

attacks.name: Confuse Ray
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Confused.

A2b-49.flavor_text: Its constant mischief and misdeeds resulted in it being bound to an Odd Keystone by a mysterious spell.

A2b-50.flavor_text: Though usually a mellow Pokémon, it will sink its sharp, poison-soaked front teeth into any that anger it, causing paralysis in the object of its ire.

ability.name: Ability: Poison Coating
ability.effect: Once during your turn, you may flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Poisoned.

A2b-51.flavor_text: The color of the poisonous saliva depends on what the Pokémon eats. Grafaiai covers its fingers in its saliva and draws patterns on trees in forests.

A2b-52.flavor_text: It swings its handmade hammer around to protect itself, but the hammer is often stolen by Pokémon that eat metal.

attacks.name: Tenacious Hammer
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 more damage.

A2b-53.flavor_text: This Pokémon will attack groups of Pawniard and Bisharp, gathering metal from them in order to create a large and sturdy hammer.

attacks.name: Terrific Thumping
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 80 more damage.

A2b-55.flavor_text: It is said that this Pokémon was born when an unknown poison Pokémon entered and inspirited an engine left at a scrap-processing factory.

attacks.name: Spinning Drift
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, during your opponent's next turn, prevent all damage from—and effects of—attacks done to this Pokémon.

A2b-56.flavor_text: It creates a gas out of poison and minerals from rocks. It then detonates the gas in its cylinders— now numbering eight—to generate energy.

attacks.name: Scintillating Surfing
attacks.effect: Flip a coin for each [M] Energy attached to this Pokémon. This attack does 50 damage for each heads.

A2b-57.flavor_text: Its body seems to be made up of 1,000 coins. This Pokémon gets along well with others and is quick to make friends with anybody.

A2b-58.flavor_text: Its incisors grow continuously throughout its life. If its incisors get too long, this Pokémon becomes unable to eat, and it starves to death.

A2b-59.flavor_text: People say that it fled from its enemies by using its small webbed hind feet to swim from island to island in Alola.

A2b-60.flavor_text: It hugely inflates its stomach and sings a mysterious melody. If you hear this melody, you’ll become sleepy right away.

ability.name: Ability: Comforting Song
ability.effect: Once during your turn, you may heal 20 damage from your Active Pokémon.

A2b-61.flavor_text: It has a very fine fur. Take care not to make it angry, or it may inflate steadily and hit with a body slam.

A2b-62.flavor_text: If this Pokémon’s sticky saliva gets on you and you don’t clean it off, an intense itch will set in. The itch won’t go away, either.

A2b-63.flavor_text: Lickilicky’s strange tongue can stretch to many times the length of its body. No one has figured out how Lickilicky’s tongue can stretch so far.

A2b-64.flavor_text: With nerves of steel, nothing can perturb it. It is more agile and active than it appears.

attacks.name: Carefree Press
attacks.effect: Heal 30 damage from this Pokémon.

A2b-66.flavor_text: If both of Buneary’s ears are rolled up, something is wrong with its body or mind. It’s a sure sign the Pokémon is in need of care.

A2b-67.flavor_text: Lopunny is constantly monitoring its surroundings. If danger approaches, this Pokémon responds with superdestructive kicks.

attacks.name: Overacceleration
attacks.effect: During your next turn, this Pokémon's Overacceleration attack does +20 damage.

A2b-68.flavor_text: Apparently Cyclizar has been allowing people to ride on its back since ancient times. Depictions of this have been found in 10,000-year-old murals.

attacks.name: Fighting Claws
attacks.effect: If your opponent's Active Pokémon is a Pokémon ex, this attack does 70 more damage.

A2b-73.flavor_text: This Pokémon uses the reflective fur lining its cape to camouflage the stem of its flower, creating the illusion that the flower is floating.

A2b-74.flavor_text: It inflates its flotation sac, keeping its face above water in order to watch for prey movement.

ability.effect: Your Active Dondozo has no Retreat Cost.
