A3-1.flavor_text: Though it may look like it’s just a bunch of eggs, it’s a proper Pokémon. Exeggcute communicates with others of its kind via telepathy, apparently.

attacks.name: Tropical Hammer
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.

A3-2.flavor_text: Blazing sunlight has brought out the true form and powers of this Pokémon.

attacks.name: Quick Attack
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 more damage.

A3-3.flavor_text: They usually live on ponds, but after an evening shower, they may appear on puddles in towns.

A3-4.flavor_text: It flaps its four wings to hover and fly freely in any direction—to and fro and sideways.

A3-5.flavor_text: With noises that could be mistaken for the rattles of maracas, it creates an upbeat rhythm, startling bird Pokémon and making them fly off in a hurry.

A3-6.flavor_text: Its strange physiology reacts to electrical energy in interesting ways. The presence of a Shelmet will cause this Pokémon to evolve.

A3-7.flavor_text: After a lost child perished in the forest, their spirit possessed a tree stump, causing the spirit’s rebirth as this Pokémon.

A3-8.flavor_text: People fear it due to a belief that it devours any who try to cut down trees in its forest, but to the Pokémon it shares its woods with, it’s kind.

A3-9.flavor_text: It sends its feathers, which are as sharp as blades, flying in attack. Its legs are strong, so its kicks are also formidable.

attacks.name: Skill Dive
attacks.effect: This attack does 10 damage to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon.

A3-10.flavor_text: It sends its feathers, which are as sharp as blades, flying in attack. Its legs are strong, so its kicks are also formidable.

A3-11.flavor_text: This narcissistic Pokémon is a clean freak. If you don’t groom it diligently, it may stop listening to you.

attacks.name: Pierce the Pain
attacks.effect: This attack does 100 damage to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon that have damage on them.

A3-13.flavor_text: Its natural enemies, like Rookidee, may flee rather than risk getting caught in its large mandibles that can snap thick tree branches.

A3-14.flavor_text: Fomantis hates having its naps interrupted. It fires off beams using energy it gathers by bathing in the sun.

attacks.name: Petal Blizzard
attacks.effect: This attack does 20 damage to each of your opponent's Pokémon.

A3-15.flavor_text: By masquerading as a bug Pokémon, it lowers the guard of actual bug Pokémon lured in by a scent of sweet flowers. Its sickles bring them down.

A3-16.flavor_text: Pokémon living in the forest eat the delicious caps on Morelull’s head. The caps regrow overnight.

attacks.name: Flickering Light
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon can't attack during your opponent's next turn.

A3-17.flavor_text: Its flickering spores lure in prey and put them to sleep. Once this Pokémon has its prey snoozing, it drains their vitality with its fingertips.

A3-18.flavor_text: Its sweat is sweet, like syrup made from boiled-down fruit. Because of this, Bounsweet was highly valued in the past, when sweeteners were scarce.

attacks.name: Double Spin
attacks.effect: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 30 damage for each heads.

A3-19.flavor_text: Steenee spreads a sweet scent that makes others feel invigorated. This same scent is popular for antiperspirants.

attacks.name: Three Kick Combo
attacks.effect: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 50 damage for each heads.

A3-20.flavor_text: This Pokémon is proud and aggressive. However, it is said that a Tsareena will instantly become calm if someone touches the crown on its calyx.

ability.name: Ability: Wimp Out
ability.effect: During your first turn, this Pokémon has no Retreat Cost.

A3-21.flavor_text: It’s nature’s cleaner—it eats anything and everything, including garbage and rotten things. The ground near its nest is always clean.

attacks.name: First Impression
attacks.effect: If this Pokémon moved from your Bench to the Active Spot this turn, this attack does 60 more damage.

A3-22.flavor_text: It will do anything to win, taking advantage of every opening and finishing opponents off with the small claws on its front legs.

attacks.name: Anchor Shot
attacks.effect: During your opponent's next turn, the Defending Pokémon can't retreat.

attacks.name: Stuck-In Tackle
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If tails, this Pokémon also does 20 damage to itself.

A3-24.flavor_text: Although it’s called a guardian deity, it’s violent enough to crush anyone it sees as an enemy.

A3-25.flavor_text: It has a brave and trustworthy nature. It fearlessly stands up to bigger and stronger foes.

A3-26.flavor_text: An ancient picture scroll shows that people were captivated by its movement as it ran through prairies.

attacks.name: Burning Bonemerang
attacks.effect: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 70 damage for each heads. If at least 1 of them is heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Burned.

A3-27.flavor_text: This Pokémon sets the bone it holds on fire and dances through the night as a way to mourn its fallen allies.

A3-28.flavor_text: Fletchinder scatters embers in tall grass where bug Pokémon might be hiding and then catches them as they come leaping out.

A3-29.flavor_text: It has top-notch flying capabilities. It flies around easily, even while carrying prey that weighs more than 220 lbs.

attacks.name: Heat Tackle
attacks.effect: This Pokémon also does 10 damage to itself.

A3-30.flavor_text: If you try too hard to get close to it, it won’t open up to you. Even if you do grow close, giving it too much affection is still a no-no.

A3-31.flavor_text: If you try too hard to get close to it, it won’t open up to you. Even if you do grow close, giving it too much affection is still a no-no.

attacks.name: Heat Tackle
attacks.effect: This Pokémon also does 10 damage to itself.

A3-32.flavor_text: Torracat will let its Trainer coddle it once they’ve grown close, but it’s a powerful, sharp-clawed Pokémon, so its Trainer gets covered in scratches.

attacks.name: Fire Fang
attacks.effect: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Burned.

attacks.name: Scar-Charged Smash
attacks.effect: If this Pokémon has damage on it, this attack does 60 more damage.

attacks.name: Kindle
attacks.effect: Discard a random Energy from both Active Pokémon.

A3-34.flavor_text: This form of Oricorio has sipped red nectar. It whips up blazing flames as it moves to the steps of its passionate dance.

A3-35.flavor_text: It taunts its prey and lures them into narrow, rocky areas where it then sprays them with toxic gas to make them dizzy and take them down.

attacks.name: Heated Poison
attacks.effect: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Poisoned and Burned.

A3-36.flavor_text: Salazzle makes its opponents light-headed with poisonous gas, then captivates them with alluring movements to turn them into loyal servants.

attacks.name: Fire Spin
attacks.effect: Discard a [R] Energy from this Pokémon.

A3-37.flavor_text: Explosive substances coat the shell on its back. Enemies that dare attack it will be blown away by an immense detonation.

A3-38.flavor_text: Life on mountains covered with deep snow has granted this Pokémon a body of ice that’s as hard as steel.

attacks.name: Spike Armor
attacks.effect: During your opponent's next turn, if this Pokémon is damaged by an attack, do 40 damage to the Attacking Pokémon.

A3-39.flavor_text: It uses large, hooked claws to cut a path through deep snow as it runs. On snowy mountains, this Sandslash is faster than any other Pokémon.

attacks.name: Call Forth Cold
attacks.effect: Take a [W] Energy from your Energy Zone and attach it to this Pokémon.

A3-40.flavor_text: After long years in the ever-snowcapped mountains of Alola, this Vulpix has gained power over ice.

attacks.name: Blizzard
attacks.effect: This attack also does 20 damage to each of your opponent's Benched Pokémon.

A3-41.flavor_text: A deity resides in the snowy mountains where this Pokémon lives. In ancient times, it was worshiped as that deity’s incarnation.

A3-42.flavor_text: It is encased in a shell that is harder than diamond. Inside, however, it is surprisingly tender.

attacks.name: Guard Press
attacks.effect: During your opponent's next turn, this Pokémon takes −20 damage from attacks.

A3-43.flavor_text: Cloyster that live in seas with harsh tidal currents grow large, sharp spikes on their shells.

A3-44.flavor_text: A smart and kindhearted Pokémon, it glides across the surface of the sea while its beautiful song echoes around it.

attacks.name: Sing
attacks.effect: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Asleep.

A3-45.flavor_text: The balloons it inflates with its nose grow larger and larger as it practices day by day.

A3-46.flavor_text: The balloons it inflates with its nose grow larger and larger as it practices day by day.

A3-47.flavor_text: It gets excited when it sees a dance it doesn’t know. This hard worker practices diligently until it can learn that dance.

ability.name: Ability: Melodious Healing
ability.effect: Once during your turn, you may heal 30 damage from each of your [W] Pokémon.

A3-48.flavor_text: To Primarina, every battle is a stage. It takes down its prey with beautiful singing and dancing.

attacks.name: Insatiable Striking
attacks.effect: During your next turn, this Pokémon's Insatiable Striking attack does +40 damage.

attacks.name: Call for Family
attacks.effect: Put 1 random Wishiwashi or Wishiwashi ex from your deck onto your Bench.

A3-50.flavor_text: Individually, they’re incredibly weak. It’s by gathering up into schools that they’re able to confront opponents.

attacks.name: School Storm
attacks.effect: This attack does 40 more damage for each of your Benched Wishiwashi or Wishiwashi ex.

A3-52.flavor_text: It forms a water bubble at the rear of its body and then covers its head with it. Meeting another Dewpider means comparing water-bubble sizes.

attacks.name: Dangerous Claws
attacks.effect: If your opponent's Active Pokémon is a Basic Pokemon, this attack does 60 more damage.

A3-53.flavor_text: It launches water bubbles with its legs, drowning prey within the bubbles. This Pokémon can then take its time to savor its meal.

ability.name: Ability: Innards Out
ability.effect: If this Pokémon is in the Active Spot and is Knocked Out by damage from an attack from your opponent's Pokémon, do 50 damage to the Attacking Pokémon.

A3-54.flavor_text: It lives in warm, shallow waters. If it encounters a foe, it will spit out its internal organs as a means to punch them.

A3-55.flavor_text: It grinds its teeth with great force to stimulate its brain. It fires the psychic energy created by this process from the protuberance on its head.

attacks.name: Spiral Drain
attacks.effect: Heal 20 damage from this Pokémon.

A3-56.flavor_text: This guardian deity of Poni Island manipulates water. Because it lives deep within a thick fog, it came to be both feared and revered.

A3-57.flavor_text: When it is angered, it immediately discharges the energy stored in the pouches in its cheeks.

attacks.name: Psychic
attacks.effect: This attack does 30 more damage for each Energy attached to your opponent's Active Pokémon.

A3-59.flavor_text: If you mistake it for a rock and step on it, it will headbutt you in anger. In addition to the pain, it will also zap you with a shock.

A3-60.flavor_text: When two Graveler fight each other, it fills the surroundings with flashes of light and sound. People call it the “fireworks of the earth.”

attacks.name: Super Zap Cannon
attacks.effect: Discard 2 [L] Energy from this Pokémon.

A3-61.flavor_text: It’s grumpy and stubborn. If you upset it, it discharges electricity from the surface of its body and growls with a voice like thunder.

A3-62.flavor_text: When spread, the frills on its head act like solar panels, generating the power behind this Pokémon’s electric moves.

A3-63.flavor_text: One Heliolisk basking in the sun with its frill outspread is all it would take to produce enough electricity to power a city.

A3-64.flavor_text: While its durable shell protects it from attacks, Charjabug strikes at enemies with jolts of electricity discharged from the tips of its jaws.

attacks.name: Disconnect
attacks.effect: During your opponent's next turn, they can't play any Item cards from their hand.

A3-65.flavor_text: It builds up electricity in its abdomen, focuses it through its jaws, and then fires the electricity off in concentrated beams.

ability.name: Ability: Safeguard
ability.effect: Prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks from your opponent's Pokémon ex.

A3-66.flavor_text: This form of Oricorio has sipped yellow nectar. It uses nimble steps to approach opponents, then knocks them out with electric punches.

attacks.name: Electrosmash
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 more damage.

A3-67.flavor_text: With the long hairs on its back, this Pokémon takes in electricity from other electric Pokémon. It stores what it absorbs in an electric sac.

attacks.name: Volt Switch
attacks.effect: Switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched [L] Pokémon.

A3-68.flavor_text: Although it’s called a guardian deity, if a person or Pokémon puts it in a bad mood, it will become a malevolent deity and attack.

attacks.name: Barrier Shove
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, during your opponent's next turn, prevent all damage from—and effects of—attacks done to this Pokémon.

A3-69.flavor_text: The broadness of its hands may be no coincidence—many scientists believe its palms became enlarged specifically for pantomiming.

attacks.name: Corner
attacks.effect: During your opponent's next turn, the Defending Pokémon can't retreat.

A3-70.flavor_text: It dwells in the darkness of caves. It uses its sharp claws to dig up gems to nourish itself.

attacks.name: Psycharge
attacks.effect: Take a [P] Energy from your Energy Zone and attach it to this Pokémon.

A3-71.flavor_text: Spoink will die if it stops bouncing. The pearl on its head amplifies its psychic powers.

A3-72.flavor_text: It can perform odd dance steps to influence foes. Its style of dancing became hugely popular overseas.

A3-73.flavor_text: The phase of the moon apparently has some effect on its power. It’s active on the night of a full moon.

A3-74.flavor_text: It loves to feed on feelings like envy and malice. Its upright horn catches the emotions of people.

attacks.name: Night Bind
attacks.effect: During your opponent's next turn, they can't take any Energy from their Energy Zone to attach to their Active Pokémon.

A3-75.flavor_text: This Pokémon developed from an abandoned doll that amassed a grudge. It is seen in dark alleys.

attacks.name: Dazzle Dance
attacks.effect: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Confused.

A3-76.flavor_text: This form of Oricorio has sipped pink nectar. It elevates its mind with the gentle steps of its dance, then unleashes its psychic energy.

attacks.name: Spiteful Dance
attacks.effect: If any of your Pokémon were Knocked Out by damage from an attack during your opponent's last turn, this attack does 60 more damage.

A3-77.flavor_text: This form of Oricorio has sipped purple nectar. It uses ethereal dance steps to call forth the spirits of the dead.

A3-78.flavor_text: Nectar and pollen are its favorite fare. In fields of flowers, it gets into skirmishes with Butterfree over food.

A3-79.flavor_text: It makes pollen puffs from pollen and nectar. The puffs’ effects depend on the type of ingredients and how much of each one is used.

ability.name: Ability: Flower Shield
ability.effect: Each of your Pokémon that has any [P] Energy attached recovers from all Special Conditions and can't be affected by any Special Conditions.

A3-80.flavor_text: Comfey picks flowers with its vine and decorates itself with them. For some reason, flowers won’t wither once they’re attached to a Comfey.

A3-81.flavor_text: If it loses its shovel, it will stick something else—like a branch—in its head to make do until it finds another shovel.

A3-82.flavor_text: The terrifying Palossand drags smaller Pokémon into its sandy body. Once its victims are trapped, it drains them of their vitality whenever it pleases.

attacks.name: Shadow Hit
attacks.effect: This attack also does 20 damage to 1 of your Pokémon.

A3-83.flavor_text: There was a scientist who peeked under Mimikyu’s old rag in the name of research. The scientist died of a mysterious disease.

attacks.name: Energy Arrow
attacks.effect: This attack does 20 damage to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon for each Energy attached to that Pokémon.

A3-84.flavor_text: It heals the wounds of people and Pokémon by sprinkling them with its sparkling scales. This guardian deity is worshiped on Akala.

attacks.name: Teleport
attacks.effect: Switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

A3-85.flavor_text: Even though its helpless, gaseous body can be blown away by the slightest breeze, it doesn’t seem to care.

attacks.name: Stiffen
attacks.effect: During your opponent's next turn, this Pokémon takes −50 damage from attacks.

A3-86.flavor_text: The king who ruled Alola in times of antiquity called it the “cocoon of the stars” and built an altar to worship it.

ability.name: Ability: Psychic Connect
ability.effect: Once during your turn, you may move all [P] Energy from 1 of your Benched [P] Pokémon to your Active Pokémon.

attacks.name: Prismatic Laser
attacks.effect: During your next turn, this Pokémon can't attack.

A3-88.flavor_text: It looks somehow pained as it rages around in search of light, which serves as its energy. It’s apparently from another world.

A3-89.flavor_text: When the memory of its departed mother brings it to tears, its cries echo mournfully within the skull it wears on its head.

A3-90.flavor_text: It grows stronger by enduring harsh training. It is a gutsy Pokémon that can withstand any attack.

attacks.name: Push Out
attacks.effect: Switch out your opponent's Active Pokémon to the Bench. (Your opponent chooses the new Active Pokémon.)

A3-91.flavor_text: It loves challenging others to tests of strength. It has the power to stop a train with a slap.

A3-92.flavor_text: When it rotates itself, it gives off light similar to the sun, thus blinding its foes.

A3-93.flavor_text: It brings its claws together and whirls around at high speed before rushing toward its prey.

A3-94.flavor_text: It loves helping out with construction projects. It loves it so much that if rain causes work to halt, it swings its log around and throws a tantrum.

A3-95.flavor_text: It shows off its muscles to Machoke and other Gurdurr. If it fails to measure up to the other Pokémon, it lies low for a little while.

ability.name: Ability: Guts
ability.effect: If this Pokémon would be Knocked Out by damage from an attack, flip a coin. If heads, this Pokémon is not Knocked Out, and its remaining HP becomes 10.

A3-96.flavor_text: Concrete mixed by Conkeldurr is much more durable than normal concrete, even when the compositions of the two materials are the same.

A3-97.flavor_text: This Pokémon punches trees and eats the berries that drop down, training itself and getting food at the same time.

attacks.name: Signs of Evolution
attacks.effect: Put a random card that evolves from Rockruff from your deck into your hand.

A3-98.flavor_text: This Pokémon is very friendly when it’s young. Its disposition becomes vicious once it matures, but it never forgets the kindness of its master.

A3-99.flavor_text: This Pokémon is very friendly when it’s young. Its disposition becomes vicious once it matures, but it never forgets the kindness of its master.

attacks.name: Crunch
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, discard a random Energy from your opponent's Active Pokémon.

A3-100.flavor_text: Lycanroc attacks its prey with its sharp claws and fangs. It loyally obeys the instructions of a Trainer it trusts.

attacks.name: Blood Fang
attacks.effect: If your opponent's Active Pokémon has more remaining HP than this Pokémon, this attack does 50 more damage.

A3-101.flavor_text: This Pokémon uses its rocky mane to slash any who approach. It will even disobey its Trainer if it dislikes the orders it was given.

A3-102.flavor_text: This Pokémon covers itself in mud that it has regurgitated. The mud won’t dry out even if it’s exposed to the sun for a long time.

attacks.name: High Horsepower
attacks.effect: This Pokémon also does 40 damage to itself.

A3-103.flavor_text: This Pokémon has been treasured not just for its physical labor but also because it produces high-quality mud used for making pottery.

ability.name: Ability: Offload Pass
ability.effect: If this Pokémon is in the Active Spot and is Knocked Out by damage from an attack from your opponent's Pokémon, move all [F] Energy from this Pokémon to 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

attacks.name: Star Drop
attacks.effect: This attack does 40 damage to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon.

A3-105.flavor_text: Places where Minior fall from the night sky are few and far between, with Alola being one of the precious few.

A3-106.flavor_text: It shows no interest in anything that isn’t fresh. If you take it shopping with you, it will help you pick out ingredients.

attacks.name: Scrounge-and-Scarf
attacks.effect: Discard a random Item card from your opponent's hand.

A3-107.flavor_text: It has an incredibly greedy personality. Its nest is filled with so much food gathered by Rattata at its direction, it can’t possibly eat it all.

A3-108.flavor_text: It’s accustomed to luxury because it used to live with Alolan royalty. As a result, it’s very picky about food.

ability.name: Ability: Fur Coat
ability.effect: This Pokémon takes −20 damage from attacks.

A3-109.flavor_text: The round face of Alolan Persian is considered to be a symbol of prosperity in the Alola region, so these Pokémon are very well cared for.

A3-110.flavor_text: There are a hundred or so of them living in Alola’s waste-disposal site. They’re all hard workers who eat a lot of trash.

attacks.name: Chemical Panic
attacks.effect: 1 Special Condition from among Asleep, Burned, Confused, Paralyzed, and Poisoned is chosen at random, and your opponent's Active Pokémon is now affected by that Special Condition. Any Special Conditions already affecting that Pokémon will not be chosen.

attacks.name: Unseen Claw
attacks.effect: If your opponent's Active Pokémon is affected by a Special Condition, this attack does 60 more damage.

A3-112.flavor_text: Swift as the wind, Absol races through fields and mountains. Its curved, bow-like horn is acutely sensitive to the warning signs of natural disasters.

A3-113.flavor_text: Its favorite places are unsanitary ones. If you leave trash lying around, you could even find one of these Pokémon living in your room.

attacks.name: Super Poison Breath
attacks.effect: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Poisoned.

A3-114.flavor_text: This Pokémon eats trash, which turns into poison inside its body. The main component of the poison depends on what sort of trash was eaten.

A3-115.flavor_text: This Pokémon wanders the seaside looking for food. It often gets electric shocks from broken Pincurchin spines that it tries to eat.

attacks.name: Spike Cannon
attacks.effect: Flip 4 coins. This attack does 20 damage for each heads.

A3-116.flavor_text: This Pokémon lives within a dome made by its own legs. Toxapex monitors its surroundings by sensing the flow of the tide through its spikes.

A3-117.flavor_text: The metal-rich geology of this Pokémon’s habitat caused it to develop steel whiskers on its head.

attacks.name: Iron Head
attacks.effect: Flip a coin until you get tails. This attack does 70 damage for each heads.

A3-118.flavor_text: Their beautiful, metallic whiskers create a sort of protective helmet on their heads, and they also function as highly precise sensors.

A3-119.flavor_text: It’s not uncommon for tunnels that appear to have formed naturally to actually be a result of Excadrill’s rampant digging.

attacks.name: Pike
attacks.effect: This attack also does 20 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon.

A3-120.flavor_text: Wearing the shell covering they stole from Shelmet, they defend themselves and attack with two lances.

attacks.name: Unlock
attacks.effect: Draw a card.

A3-121.flavor_text: Once it absorbs a key’s metal ions, it discards the key without a second thought. However, it will hang on to keys it favors for decades.

attacks.name: Sol Breaker
attacks.effect: This Pokémon also does 10 damage to itself.

ability.name: Ability: Rising Road
ability.effect: Once during your turn, if this Pokémon is on your Bench, you may switch it with your Active Pokémon.

attacks.name: Silver Cannon
attacks.effect: If your opponent's Active Pokémon has an Ability, this attack does 40 more damage.

A3-123.flavor_text: It synchronizes its consciousness with others to understand their feelings. This faculty makes it useful for taking care of people.

attacks.name: Berserk
attacks.effect: If any of your Benched Pokémon have damage on them, this attack does 50 more damage.

A3-124.flavor_text: The mountains it calls home are nearly two miles in height. On rare occasions, it descends to play with the children living in the towns below.

A3-125.flavor_text: They learn to fight by smashing their head scales together. The dueling strengthens both their skills and their spirits.

A3-126.flavor_text: The scaleless, scarred parts of its body are signs of its strength. It shows them off to defeated opponents.

attacks.name: Clanging Scales
attacks.effect: During your opponent's next turn, this Pokémon takes +30 damage from attacks.

A3-127.flavor_text: It clatters its tail scales to unnerve opponents. This Pokémon will battle only those who stand steadfast in the face of this display.

attacks.name: Rising Lunge
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 50 more damage.

A3-128.flavor_text: When Tauros begins whipping itself with its tails, it’s a warning that the Pokémon is about to charge with astounding speed.

A3-129.flavor_text: It can’t stop itself from chasing moving things, and it runs in a circle, chasing its own tail.

attacks.name: Energy Assist
attacks.effect: Take a [C] Energy from your Energy Zone and attach it to 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

A3-130.flavor_text: It dislikes dirty places. It often searches for a comfortable place in which to groom itself.

A3-131.flavor_text: This Pokémon is normally calm, but once it enters battle, its hormonal balance changes and it becomes aggressive.

attacks.name: Justified Press
attacks.effect: If your opponent's Active Pokémon is a [D] Pokémon, this attack does 30 more damage.

A3-132.flavor_text: Its elegant finishing moves—performed by nimbly leaping around using its wings—are polished in the forest where it was born and raised.

A3-133.flavor_text: It pecks at trees with its hard beak. You can get some idea of its mood or condition from the rhythm of its pecking.

A3-134.flavor_text: It can bend the tip of its beak to produce over a hundred different cries at will.

A3-135.flavor_text: They smack beaks with others of their kind to communicate. The strength and number of hits tell each other how they feel.

A3-136.flavor_text: Its stomach fills most of its torso. It wanders the same path every day, searching for fresh food.

A3-137.flavor_text: Once it finds signs of prey, it will patiently stake out the location, waiting until the sun goes down.

A3-138.flavor_text: Its fluffy fur is a delight to pet, but carelessly reaching out to touch this Pokémon could result in painful retaliation.

attacks.name: Tantrum
attacks.effect: This Pokémon is now Confused.

A3-139.flavor_text: Once it accepts you as a friend, it tries to show its affection with a hug. Letting it do that is dangerous—it could easily shatter your bones.

attacks.name: Primate’s Trap
attacks.effect: During your opponent's next turn, attacks used by the Defending Pokémon cost 1 [C] more, and its Retreat Cost is 1 [C] more.

A3-140.flavor_text: People used to mistake Oranguru for a human when they saw it issue command after command to the other Pokémon of the forest.

ability.name: Ability: Comatose
ability.effect: As long as this Pokémon is in the Active Spot, whenever you attach an Energy from your Energy Zone to it, it is now Asleep.

A3-141.flavor_text: Komala spends its entire life sleeping. It feeds on leaves that contain a potent poison only Komala can break down.

attacks.name: Tropical Hammer
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.

A3-156.flavor_text: Blazing sunlight has brought out the true form and powers of this Pokémon.

A3-157.flavor_text: Pokémon living in the forest eat the delicious caps on Morelull’s head. The caps regrow overnight.

attacks.name: Three Kick Combo
attacks.effect: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 50 damage for each heads.

A3-158.flavor_text: This Pokémon is proud and aggressive. However, it is said that a Tsareena will instantly become calm if someone touches the crown on its calyx.

attacks.name: Stuck-In Tackle
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If tails, this Pokémon also does 20 damage to itself.

A3-159.flavor_text: Although it’s called a guardian deity, it’s violent enough to crush anyone it sees as an enemy.

attacks.name: Burning Bonemerang
attacks.effect: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 70 damage for each heads. If at least 1 of them is heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Burned.

A3-160.flavor_text: This Pokémon sets the bone it holds on fire and dances through the night as a way to mourn its fallen allies.

attacks.name: Fire Spin
attacks.effect: Discard a [R] Energy from this Pokémon.

A3-161.flavor_text: Explosive substances coat the shell on its back. Enemies that dare attack it will be blown away by an immense detonation.

attacks.name: Call Forth Cold
attacks.effect: Take a [W] Energy from your Energy Zone and attach it to this Pokémon.

A3-162.flavor_text: After long years in the ever-snowcapped mountains of Alola, this Vulpix has gained power over ice.

ability.name: Ability: Innards Out
ability.effect: If this Pokémon is in the Active Spot and is Knocked Out by damage from an attack from your opponent's Pokémon, do 50 damage to the Attacking Pokémon.

A3-210.flavor_text: There is a plant seed on its back right from the day this Pokémon is born. The seed slowly grows larger.

A3-211.flavor_text: When the bulb on its back grows large, it appears to lose the ability to stand on its hind legs.

attacks.name: Mega Drain
attacks.effect: Heal 30 damage from this Pokémon.

A3-212.flavor_text: Its plant blooms when it is absorbing solar energy. It stays on the move to seek sunlight.

A3-213.flavor_text: Though it may look like it’s just a bunch of eggs, it’s a proper Pokémon. Exeggcute communicates with others of its kind via telepathy, apparently.

attacks.name: Psychic
attacks.effect: This attack does 20 more damage for each Energy attached to your opponent's Active Pokémon.

A3-214.flavor_text: Each of Exeggutor’s three heads is thinking different thoughts. The three don’t seem to be very interested in one another.

A3-215.flavor_text: When it retracts its long neck into its shell, it squirts out water with vigorous force.

A3-216.flavor_text: It is recognized as a symbol of longevity. If its shell has algae on it, that Wartortle is very old.

attacks.name: Hydro Pump
attacks.effect: If this Pokémon has at least 2 extra [W] Energy attached, this attack does 60 more damage.

A3-217.flavor_text: It crushes its foe under its heavy body to cause fainting. In a pinch, it will withdraw inside its shell.

A3-218.flavor_text: If you visit a beach at the end of summer, you’ll be able to see groups of Staryu lighting up in a steady rhythm.

A3-219.flavor_text: This Pokémon has an organ known as its core. The organ glows in seven colors when Starmie is unleashing its potent psychic powers.

attacks.name: Astonish
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent reveals a random card from their hand and shuffles it into their deck.

A3-220.flavor_text: It wraps its opponent in its gas-like body, slowly weakening its prey by poisoning it through the skin.

A3-221.flavor_text: It likes to lurk in the dark and tap shoulders with a gaseous hand. Its touch causes endless shuddering.

attacks.name: Bother
attacks.effect: Your opponent can't use any Supporter cards from their hand during their next turn.

A3-222.flavor_text: To steal the life of its target, it slips into the prey’s shadow and silently waits for an opportunity.

A3-223.flavor_text: Its whole body is composed of muscles. Even though it’s the size of a human child, it can hurl 100 grown-ups.

A3-224.flavor_text: Its muscular body is so powerful, it must wear a power-save belt to be able to regulate its motions.

attacks.name: Power Press
attacks.effect: If this Pokémon has at least 2 extra [F] Energy attached, this attack does 50 more damage.

A3-225.flavor_text: It quickly swings its four arms to rock its opponents with ceaseless punches and chops from all angles.

attacks.name: Growl
attacks.effect: During your opponent's next turn, attacks used by the Defending Pokémon do -20 damage.

A3-226.flavor_text: When the memory of its departed mother brings it to tears, its cries echo mournfully within the skull it wears on its head.

A3-227.flavor_text: This Pokémon overcame its sorrow to evolve a sturdy new body. Marowak faces its opponents bravely, using a bone as a weapon.

A3-228.flavor_text: It hugely inflates its stomach and sings a mysterious melody. If you hear this melody, you’ll become sleepy right away.

ability.name: Ability: Comforting Song
ability.effect: Once during your turn, you may heal 20 damage from your Active Pokémon.

A3-229.flavor_text: It has a very fine fur. Take care not to make it angry, or it may inflate steadily and hit with a body slam.

attacks.name: Giant Bloom
attacks.effect: Heal 30 damage from this Pokémon.

attacks.name: Tropical Swing
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 40 more damage.

attacks.name: Hydro Bazooka
attacks.effect: If this Pokémon has at least 2 extra [W] Energy attached, this attack does 60 more damage.

ability.name: Ability: Shadowy Spellbind
ability.effect: As long as this Pokémon is in the Active Spot, your opponent can't use any Supporter cards from their hand.

attacks.name: Bonemerang
attacks.effect: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 80 damage for each heads.

attacks.name: Sleepy Song
attacks.effect: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Asleep.

ability.name: Ability: Psychic Connect
ability.effect: Once during your turn, you may move all [P] Energy from 1 of your Benched [P] Pokémon to your Active Pokémon.

attacks.name: Sol Breaker
attacks.effect: This Pokémon also does 10 damage to itself.

ability.name: Ability: Rising Road
ability.effect: Once during your turn, if this Pokémon is on your Bench, you may switch it with your Active Pokémon.

A3a-1.flavor_text: The leaves on its head grow right back even if they fall out. These bitter leaves refresh those who eat them.

A3a-2.flavor_text: The fragrance of the garland on its head has a relaxing effect, but taking care of it is very difficult.

attacks.name: Fury Attack
attacks.effect: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 10 damage for each heads.

A3a-3.flavor_text: It sends its feathers, which are as sharp as blades, flying in attack. Its legs are strong, so its kicks are also formidable.

attacks.name: Skill Dive
attacks.effect: This attack does 20 damage to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon.

A3a-4.flavor_text: This narcissistic Pokémon is a clean freak. If you don’t groom it diligently, it may stop listening to you.

attacks.name: Sniping Arrow
attacks.effect: This attack does 70 damage to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon.

A3a-5.flavor_text: It nocks its arrow quills and shoots them at opponents. When it simply can’t afford to miss, it tugs the vine on its head to improve its focus.

attacks.name: Big Beat
attacks.effect: During your next turn, this Pokémon can't use Big Beat.

attacks.name: Jump Blues
attacks.effect: This attack also does 20 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon.

A3a-7.flavor_text: A life-form that lives in another world, its body is thin and supple, but it also possesses great power.

A3a-8.flavor_text: This Ultra Beast’s body, which is as thin as paper, is like a sharpened sword.

attacks.name: Beat Punk
attacks.effect: This Pokémon also does 70 damage to itself.

A3a-9.flavor_text: It slithers toward people. Then, without warning, it triggers the explosion of its own head. It’s apparently one kind of Ultra Beast.

A3a-10.flavor_text: If it builds up enough speed swimming, it can jump out above the waves and glide for over 300 feet.

A3a-11.flavor_text: It won’t attack while it’s alone—not even if it spots prey. Instead, it waits for other Carvanha to join it, and then the Pokémon attack as a group.

attacks.name: Crunch
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, discard a random Energy from your opponent's Active Pokémon.

A3a-12.flavor_text: As soon as it catches the scent of prey, Sharpedo will jet seawater from its backside, hurtling toward the target to attack at 75 mph.

A3a-13.flavor_text: The extension and contraction of its muscles generates electricity. It glows when in trouble.

A3a-14.flavor_text: Strong electricity courses through the tips of its sharp claws. A light scratch causes fainting in foes.

ability.name: Ability: Intimidating Fang
ability.effect: As long as this Pokémon is in the Active Spot, attacks used by your opponent's Active Pokémon do −20 damage.

A3a-15.flavor_text: It can see clearly through walls to track down its prey and seek its lost young.

A3a-16.flavor_text: When thunderclouds cover the sky, it will appear. It can catch lightning with its mane and store the electricity.

A3a-17.flavor_text: When this ill-tempered Pokémon runs wild, it shoots lightning from its mane in all directions.

A3a-18.flavor_text: As Emolga flutters through the air, it crackles with electricity. This Pokémon is cute, but it can cause a lot of trouble.

attacks.name: Plasma Hurricane
attacks.effect: Take a [L] Energy from your Energy Zone and attach it to this Pokémon.

attacks.name: Electronica
attacks.effect: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Confused.

A3a-20.flavor_text: Although it’s alien to this world and a danger here, it’s apparently a common organism in the world where it normally lives.

ability.name: Ability: Thunderclap Flash
ability.effect: At the end of your first turn, take a [L] Energy from your Energy Zone and attach it to this Pokémon.

A3a-21.flavor_text: It electrifies its claws and tears its opponents apart with them. Even if they dodge its attack, they’ll be electrocuted by the flying sparks.

A3a-22.flavor_text: It is said that happiness will come to those who see a gathering of Clefairy dancing under a full moon.

A3a-23.flavor_text: A timid fairy Pokémon that is rarely seen, it will run and hide the moment it senses people.

A3a-24.flavor_text: With a voice like a human child’s, it cries out to lure adults deep into the forest, getting them lost among the trees.

A3a-25.flavor_text: People fear it due to a belief that it devours any who try to cut down trees in its forest, but to the Pokémon it shares its woods with, it’s kind.

A3a-26.flavor_text: It scatters spores that flicker and glow. Anyone seeing these lights falls into a deep slumber.

ability.name: Ability: Illuminate
ability.effect: Once during your turn, you may put a random Pokémon from your deck into your hand.

A3a-27.flavor_text: Its flickering spores lure in prey and put them to sleep. Once this Pokémon has its prey snoozing, it drains their vitality with its fingertips.

attacks.name: Trip Over
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 more damage.

A3a-28.flavor_text: It never skips its daily yoga training. It heightens its inner strength through meditation.

A3a-29.flavor_text: Through yoga training, it gained the psychic power to predict its foe’s next move.

A3a-30.flavor_text: It moves while spinning around on its single foot. Some Baltoy have been seen spinning on their heads.

ability.name: Ability: Heal Block
ability.effect: Pokémon (both yours and your opponent's) can't be healed.

A3a-31.flavor_text: This mysterious Pokémon started life as an ancient clay figurine made over 20,000 years ago.

A3a-32.flavor_text: This Pokémon is very friendly when it’s young. Its disposition becomes vicious once it matures, but it never forgets the kindness of its master.

attacks.name: Lycanfang
attacks.effect: Discard a [F] Energy from this Pokémon.

attacks.name: Let’s Throw
attacks.effect: If Passimian is on your Bench, this attack does 40 more damage.

A3a-34.flavor_text: This Pokémon battles by throwing hard berries. It won’t obey a Trainer who throws Poké Balls without skill.

A3a-35.flavor_text: If it loses its shovel, it will stick something else—like a branch—in its head to make do until it finds another shovel.

attacks.name: Sand Tomb
attacks.effect: During your opponent's next turn, the Defending Pokémon can't retreat.

A3a-36.flavor_text: The terrifying Palossand drags smaller Pokémon into its sandy body. Once its victims are trapped, it drains them of their vitality whenever it pleases.

attacks.name: Meddle
attacks.effect: Discard a random Pokémon Tool card from your opponent's hand.

A3a-37.flavor_text: It’s accustomed to luxury because it used to live with Alolan royalty. As a result, it’s very picky about food.

attacks.name: Fake Out
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

A3a-38.flavor_text: The round face of Alolan Persian is considered to be a symbol of prosperity in the Alola region, so these Pokémon are very well cared for.

A3a-39.flavor_text: It submerges itself in sand and moves as if swimming. This wise behavior keeps its enemies from finding it and maintains its temperature.

A3a-40.flavor_text: Protected by thin membranes, their eyes can see even in the dead of night. They live in groups of a few individuals.

attacks.name: Poaching Fangs
attacks.effect: Flip 3 coins. For each heads, a card is chosen at random from your opponent's hand. Your opponent reveals that card and shuffles it into their deck.

A3a-41.flavor_text: After clamping down with its powerful jaws, it twists its body around to rip its prey in half.

attacks.name: New Wave
attacks.effect: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Poisoned.

ability.name: Ability: More Poison
ability.effect: Your opponent's Active Pokémon takes +10 damage from being Poisoned.

A3a-42.flavor_text: A life-form from another world, it was dubbed a UB and is thought to produce a strong neurotoxin.

attacks.name: Grindcore
attacks.effect: Flip a coin until you get tails. For each heads, discard a random Energy from your opponent's Active Pokémon.

attacks.name: 2-Step
attacks.effect: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 20 damage for each heads.

A3a-44.flavor_text: This Ultra Beast is well enough liked to be chosen as a first partner in its own world.

attacks.name: Electro House
attacks.effect: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Poisoned.

A3a-45.flavor_text: It stores hundreds of liters of poisonous liquid inside its body. It is one of the organisms known as UBs.

attacks.name: Happened to Headbutt
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.

A3a-46.flavor_text: The metal-rich geology of this Pokémon’s habitat caused it to develop steel whiskers on its head.

attacks.name: Triplet Headbutt
attacks.effect: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 60 damage for each heads.

A3a-48.flavor_text: It eats iron ore—and sometimes railroad tracks— to build up the steel armor that protects its body.

A3a-49.flavor_text: It loves iron ore. Groups of them fight for territory by bashing one another with their steel bodies.

attacks.name: Giga Impact
attacks.effect: During your next turn, this Pokémon can't attack.

A3a-50.flavor_text: Aggron has a horn sharp enough to perforate thick iron sheets. It brings down its opponents by ramming into them horn first.

A3a-51.flavor_text: It absorbs the iron it finds in the rock while clinging to the ceiling. It shoots spikes when in danger.

ability.name: Ability: Steel Spikes
ability.effect: If this Pokémon is in the Active Spot and is damaged by an attack from your opponent's Pokémon, do 20 damage to the Attacking Pokémon.

A3a-52.flavor_text: This Pokémon scrapes its spikes across rocks, and then uses the tips of its feelers to absorb the nutrients it finds within the stone.

attacks.name: Brass Rock
attacks.effect: During your opponent's next turn, this Pokémon takes −20 damage from attacks.

A3a-53.flavor_text: It appeared from an Ultra Wormhole. Each one appears to be made up of many life-forms stacked one on top of each other.

A3a-54.flavor_text: This Pokémon is courageous but also cautious. It uses the soft fur covering its face to collect information about its surroundings.

A3a-55.flavor_text: Herdier is a very smart and friendly Pokémon. So much so that there’s a theory that Herdier was the first Pokémon to partner with people.

ability.name: Ability: Guard Dog Visage
ability.effect: As long as this Pokémon is in the Active Spot, attacks used by your opponent's Active Pokémon cost 1 [C] more.

A3a-56.flavor_text: These Pokémon seem to enjoy living with humans. Even a Stoutland caught in the wild will warm up to people in about three days.

A3a-57.flavor_text: Its fluffy fur is a delight to pet, but carelessly reaching out to touch this Pokémon could result in painful retaliation.

attacks.name: Superpowered Hug
attacks.effect: Flip 2 coins. If both of them are heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is Knocked Out.

A3a-58.flavor_text: Once it accepts you as a friend, it tries to show its affection with a hug. Letting it do that is dangerous—it could easily shatter your bones.

A3a-59.flavor_text: It normally spends its time meditating in the treetops. It throws Poké Balls and gives other Pokémon orders as it pleases.

attacks.name: Quick Blow
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 more damage.

A3a-60.flavor_text: A Pokémon weapon developed for a specific mission, it went berserk during an experiment, so it was cryogenically frozen.

attacks.name: Brave Buddies
attacks.effect: If you played a Supporter card from your hand during this turn, this attack does 50 more damage.

A3a-61.flavor_text: This is its form once it has awakened and evolved. Freed from its heavy mask, its speed is greatly increased.

attacks.name: Moombahton
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.

ability.name: Ability: Ultra Thrusters
ability.effect: Once during your turn, you may switch your Active Ultra Beast with 1 of your Benched Ultra Beasts.

A3a-62.flavor_text: One of the dangerous UBs, high energy readings can be detected coming from both of its huge arms.

attacks.name: Fury Attack
attacks.effect: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 10 damage for each heads.

A3a-70.flavor_text: It sends its feathers, which are as sharp as blades, flying in attack. Its legs are strong, so its kicks are also formidable.

attacks.effect: If you played a Supporter card from your hand during this turn, this attack does 50 more damage.

A3a-74.flavor_text: This is its form once it has awakened and evolved. Freed from its heavy mask, its speed is greatly increased.

attacks.name: Moombahton

attacks.effect: During your next turn, this Pokémon can't use Big Beat.

attacks.name: Big Beat
attacks.effect: During your next turn, this Pokémon can't use Big Beat.

A3a-89.flavor_text: It has a brave and trustworthy nature. It fearlessly stands up to bigger and stronger foes.

attacks.name: Heat Tackle
attacks.effect: This Pokémon also does 20 damage to itself.

A3a-90.flavor_text: An ancient picture scroll shows that people were captivated by its movement as it ran through prairies.

A3a-91.flavor_text: It secretes flexible bubbles from its chest and back. The bubbles reduce the damage it would otherwise take when attacked.

A3a-92.flavor_text: It can throw bubble-covered pebbles with precise control, hitting empty cans up to a hundred feet away.

ability.name: Ability: Water Shuriken
ability.effect: Once during your turn, you may do 20 damage to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon.

A3a-93.flavor_text: It creates throwing stars out of compressed water. When it spins them and throws them at high speed, these stars can split metal in two.

attacks.name: Psychic
attacks.effect: This attack does 20 more damage for each Energy attached to your opponent's Active Pokémon.

A3a-94.flavor_text: Its strange cries sound like human language. There are some musicians who compose songs for Jynx to sing.

A3a-95.flavor_text: A common sight in forests and woods. It flaps its wings at ground level to kick up blinding sand.

A3a-96.flavor_text: The claws on its feet are well developed. It can carry prey such as an Exeggcute to its nest over 60 miles away.

ability.name: Ability: Drive Off
ability.effect: Once during your turn, you may switch out your opponent's Active Pokémon to the Bench. (Your opponent chooses the new Active Pokémon.)

A3a-97.flavor_text: When hunting, it skims the surface of water at high speed to pick off unwary prey such as Magikarp.

attacks.name: Primal Wingbeat
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent shuffles their Active Pokémon back into their deck.

A3a-98.flavor_text: This is a ferocious Pokémon from ancient times. Apparently even modern technology is incapable of producing a perfectly restored specimen.

attacks.name: Powerful Bloom
attacks.effect: Flip a coin for each Energy attached to this Pokémon. This attack does 50 damage for each heads.

attacks.name: Inferno Onrush
attacks.effect: This Pokémon also does 20 damage to itself.

ability.name: Ability: Primeval Law
ability.effect: Your opponent can't play any Pokémon from their hand to evolve their Active Pokémon.

attacks.name: Scattering Cyclone
attacks.effect: This attack does 20 more damage for each of your opponent's Benched Pokémon.

attacks.name: New Wave
attacks.effect: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Poisoned.

ability.name: Ability: More Poison
ability.effect: Your opponent's Active Pokémon takes +10 damage from being Poisoned.

A3a-103.flavor_text: A life-form from another world, it was dubbed a UB and is thought to produce a strong neurotoxin.

A3b-1.flavor_text: It lives in tropical jungles. The bunch of fruit around its neck is delicious. The fruit grows twice a year.

attacks.name: Leaf Blast
attacks.effect: This attack does 20 more damage for each [G] Energy attached to this Pokémon.

A3b-2.flavor_text: When you see Leafeon asleep in a patch of sunshine, you’ll know it is using photosynthesis to produce clean air.

A3b-3.flavor_text: Its sweat is sweet, like syrup made from boiled-down fruit. Because of this, Bounsweet was highly valued in the past, when sweeteners were scarce.

A3b-4.flavor_text: Steenee spreads a sweet scent that makes others feel invigorated. This same scent is popular for antiperspirants.

attacks.name: Kick Down
attacks.effect: Your opponent reveals a random card from their hand and shuffles it into their deck.

A3b-5.flavor_text: This Pokémon is proud and aggressive. However, it is said that a Tsareena will instantly become calm if someone touches the crown on its calyx.

A3b-6.flavor_text: It grows by eating the flesh of an apple. Applin uses its own body fluid to strengthen the apple’s skin and prevent it from rotting.

attacks.name: Sweets Relay
attacks.effect: If 1 of your Pokémon used Sweets Relay during your last turn, this attack does 30 more damage.

A3b-7.flavor_text: It feeds mainly on sweet apples. It will also eat small bug Pokémon that are attracted by its sweet nectar.

attacks.name: Assisting Heater
attacks.effect: Take a [R] Energy from your Energy Zone and attach it to 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

A3b-8.flavor_text: Inhaled air is carried to its flame sac, heated, and exhaled as fire that reaches over 3,000 degrees Fahrenheit.

attacks.name: Fire Spin
attacks.effect: Discard 2 [R] Energy from this Pokémon.

ability.name: Ability: Combust
ability.effect: Once during your turn, you may attach a [R] Energy from your discard pile to this Pokémon. If you do, do 20 damage to this Pokémon.

attacks.name: Flamethrower
attacks.effect: Discard a [R] Energy from this Pokémon.

A3b-10.flavor_text: It burns coal inside its shell for energy. It blows out black soot if it is endangered.

A3b-11.flavor_text: If you try too hard to get close to it, it won’t open up to you. Even if you do grow close, giving it too much affection is still a no-no.

A3b-12.flavor_text: Torracat will let its Trainer coddle it once they’ve grown close, but it’s a powerful, sharp-clawed Pokémon, so its Trainer gets covered in scratches.

attacks.name: Darkest Lariat
attacks.effect: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 100 damage for each heads.

A3b-13.flavor_text: Although it’s rough mannered and egotistical, it finds beating down unworthy opponents boring. It gets motivated for stronger opponents.

attacks.name: Ember
attacks.effect: Discard a [R] Energy from this Pokémon.

A3b-14.flavor_text: It taunts its prey and lures them into narrow, rocky areas where it then sprays them with toxic gas to make them dizzy and take them down.

A3b-15.flavor_text: Salazzle makes its opponents light-headed with poisonous gas, then captivates them with alluring movements to turn them into loyal servants.

attacks.name: Hyper Whirlpool
attacks.effect: Flip a coin until you get tails. For each heads, discard a random Energy from your opponent's Active Pokémon.

A3b-16.flavor_text: It lives close to water. Its long tail is ridged with a fin, which is often mistaken for a mermaid’s.

attacks.name: Ice Blade
attacks.effect: This attack does 50 damage to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon.

A3b-17.flavor_text: It can control its body temperature at will. This enables it to freeze the moisture in the atmosphere, creating flurries of diamond dust.

attacks.name: Sweets Relay
attacks.effect: If 1 of your Pokémon used Sweets Relay during your last turn, this attack does 20 more damage.

A3b-18.flavor_text: Unable to survive in hot areas, it makes itself comfortable by breathing out air cold enough to cause snow. It burrows into the snow to sleep.

attacks.name: Sweets Relay
attacks.effect: If 1 of your Pokémon used Sweets Relay during your last turn, this attack does 20 more damage.

A3b-19.flavor_text: By drinking pure water, it grows its icy body. This Pokémon can be hard to find on days with warm, sunny weather.

attacks.name: Double Spin
attacks.effect: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 80 damage for each heads.

A3b-20.flavor_text: When its anger reaches a breaking point, this Pokémon unleashes a fierce blizzard that freezes every creature around it, be they friend or foe.

attacks.name: Water Pulse
attacks.effect: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Asleep.

A3b-21.flavor_text: It gently holds injured and weak Pokémon in its fins. Its special membrane heals their wounds.

A3b-22.flavor_text: The balloons it inflates with its nose grow larger and larger as it practices day by day.

A3b-23.flavor_text: It gets excited when it sees a dance it doesn’t know. This hard worker practices diligently until it can learn that dance.

attacks.name: Hydro Pump
attacks.effect: If this Pokémon has at least 1 extra [W] Energy attached, this attack does 40 more damage.

attacks.name: Sparkling Aria
attacks.effect: Heal 20 damage from this Pokémon.

attacks.name: Beginning Bolt
attacks.effect: If this Pokémon evolved during this turn, this attack does 20 more damage.

A3b-25.flavor_text: It concentrates the weak electric charges emitted by its cells and launches wicked lightning bolts.

attacks.name: Jolt
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

A3b-26.flavor_text: Joltik can be found clinging to other Pokémon. It’s soaking up static electricity because it can’t produce a charge on its own.

attacks.name: Electric Shock
attacks.effect: Discard all Energy attached to this Pokémon. Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

A3b-27.flavor_text: It launches electrified fur from its abdomen as its means of attack. Opponents hit by the fur could be in for three full days and nights of paralysis.

attacks.name: Energy Crush
attacks.effect: This attack does 20 damage for each Energy attached to all of your opponent's Pokémon.

A3b-28.flavor_text: The tip of its forked tail quivers when it is predicting its opponent’s next move.

A3b-29.flavor_text: While inside a cave, if you look up and see lots of heart-shaped marks lining the walls, it’s evidence that Woobat live there.

A3b-30.flavor_text: Emitting powerful sound waves tires it out. Afterward, it won’t be able to fly for a little while.

attacks.name: Sweets Relay
attacks.effect: If 1 of your Pokémon used Sweets Relay during your last turn, this attack does 20 more damage.

A3b-31.flavor_text: It eats its own weight in sugar every day. If it doesn’t get enough sugar, it becomes incredibly grumpy.

attacks.name: Sweets Relay
attacks.effect: If 1 of your Pokémon used Sweets Relay during your last turn, this attack does 60 more damage.

A3b-32.flavor_text: By taking in a person’s scent, it can sniff out their mental and physical condition. It’s hoped that this skill will have many medical applications.

attacks.name: Evoharmony
attacks.effect: This attack does 30 more damage for each Evolution Pokémon on your Bench.

A3b-33.flavor_text: This Pokémon uses its ribbonlike feelers to send a soothing aura into its opponents, erasing their hostility.

ability.name: Ability: Happy Ribbon
ability.effect: Once during your turn, when you play this Pokémon from your hand to evolve 1 of your Pokémon, you may draw 2 cards.

attacks.name: Try to Imitate
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, choose 1 of your opponent's Active Pokémon's attacks and use it as this attack.

A3b-35.flavor_text: This Pokémon lives in dark places untouched by sunlight. When it appears before humans, it hides itself under a cloth that resembles a Pikachu.

attacks.name: Sweets Relay
attacks.effect: If 1 of your Pokémon used Sweets Relay during your last turn, this attack does 20 more damage.

A3b-36.flavor_text: This Pokémon was born from sweet-smelling particles in the air. Its body is made of cream.

attacks.name: Sweets Overload
attacks.effect: This attack does 40 damage for each time your Pokémon used Sweets Relay during this game.

A3b-37.flavor_text: When it trusts a Trainer, it will treat them to berries it’s decorated with cream.

A3b-38.flavor_text: Its two whiskers provide a sensitive radar. Even in muddy waters, it can detect its prey’s location.

attacks.name: Earthquake
attacks.effect: This attack also does 10 damage to each of your Benched Pokémon.

A3b-39.flavor_text: It is extremely protective of its territory. If any foe approaches, it attacks using vicious tremors.

attacks.name: Kick Shot
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.

A3b-40.flavor_text: In one minute, a well-trained Mienfoo can chop with its arms more than 100 times.

A3b-41.flavor_text: When Mienshao comes across a truly challenging opponent, it will lighten itself by biting off the fur on its arms.

attacks.name: Hard Roll
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, during your opponent's next turn, prevent all damage from—and effects of—attacks done to this Pokémon.

A3b-42.flavor_text: When beset by attackers, Carbink wipes them all out by firing high-energy beams from the gems embedded in its body.

attacks.name: Dark Binding
attacks.effect: If the Defending Pokémon is a Basic Pokémon, it can't attack during your opponent's next turn.

A3b-43.flavor_text: When exposed to the moon’s aura, the rings on its body glow faintly and it gains a mysterious power.

A3b-44.flavor_text: Materials from gems it has eaten float to the surface of its body and can form an infinite number of patterns among individuals.

attacks.name: Playful Knockdown
attacks.effect: Discard all Pokémon Tools from your opponent's Active Pokémon.

A3b-45.flavor_text: Opponents that get drawn in by its adorable behavior come away with stinging scratches from its claws and stinging pride from its laughter.

A3b-46.flavor_text: Don’t be fooled by its gorgeous fur and elegant figure. This is a moody and vicious Pokémon.

attacks.name: Powerful Vise
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

A3b-47.flavor_text: It uses its docile-looking face to lull foes into complacency, then bites with its huge, relentless jaws.

attacks.name: Bristling Spikes
attacks.effect: During your opponent's next turn, if this Pokémon is damaged by an attack, do 30 damage to the Attacking Pokémon.

A3b-48.flavor_text: With the long hairs on its back, this Pokémon takes in electricity from other electric Pokémon. It stores what it absorbs in an electric sac.

A3b-49.flavor_text: It dissolves and eats metal. Circulating liquid metal within its body is how it generates energy.

A3b-50.flavor_text: At the end of its life-span, Melmetal will rust and fall apart. The small shards left behind will eventually be reborn as Meltan.

A3b-51.flavor_text: It sheds many layers of skin as it grows larger. During this process, it is protected by a rapid waterfall.

A3b-52.flavor_text: They say that if it emits an aura from its whole body, the weather will begin to change instantly.

attacks.name: Giga Impact
attacks.effect: During your next turn, this Pokémon can't attack.

attacks.name: Dragon Breath
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

A3b-54.flavor_text: The mountains it calls home are nearly two miles in height. On rare occasions, it descends to play with the children living in the towns below.

attacks.name: Collect
attacks.effect: Draw a card.

A3b-55.flavor_text: Its ability to evolve into many forms allows it to adapt smoothly and perfectly to any environment.

ability.name: Ability: Veevee ’volve
ability.effect: This Pokémon can evolve into any Pokémon that evolves from Eevee if you play it from your hand onto this Pokémon. (This Pokémon can't evolve during your first turn or the turn you play it.)

attacks.name: Flop-Down Punch
attacks.effect: This Pokémon is now Asleep.

ability.name: Ability: Full-Mouth Manner
ability.effect: At the end of your turn, if this Pokémon is in the Active Spot, heal 20 damage from it.

attacks.name: Double Hit
attacks.effect: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 20 damage for each heads.

A3b-58.flavor_text: As it did more and more with its tail, its hands became clumsy. It makes its nest high in the treetops.

ability.name: Ability: Catching Tail
ability.effect: Once during your turn, you may put a random Pokémon Tool card from your deck into your hand.

A3b-59.flavor_text: It uses its tails for everything. If it wraps both of its tails around you and gives you a squeeze, that’s proof it really likes you.

attacks.name: Tone-Deaf
attacks.effect: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Confused.

A3b-60.flavor_text: It mimics the cries of other Pokémon to trick them into thinking it’s one of them. This way they won’t attack it.

attacks.name: Do the Wave
attacks.effect: This attack does 20 damage for each of your Benched Pokémon.

A3b-61.flavor_text: This Pokémon has a kind heart. By touching with its feelers, Audino can gauge other creatures’ feelings and physical conditions.

A3b-62.flavor_text: The way it brushes away grime with its tail can be helpful when cleaning. But its focus on spotlessness can make cleaning more of a hassle.

A3b-63.flavor_text: Its body secretes oil that this Pokémon spreads over its nest as a coating to protect it from dust. Cinccino won’t tolerate even a speck of the stuff.

A3b-64.flavor_text: It stores berries in its cheeks. When there are no berries to be found, Skwovet will stuff pebbles into its cheeks to stave off its cravings.

attacks.name: Enhanced Fang
attacks.effect: If this Pokémon has a Pokémon Tool attached, this attack does 50 more damage.

A3b-65.flavor_text: This Pokémon makes off with heaps of fallen berries by wrapping them in its tail, which is roughly twice the length of its body.

attacks.name: Leaf Blast
attacks.effect: This attack does 20 more damage for each [G] Energy attached to this Pokémon.

A3b-70.flavor_text: When you see Leafeon asleep in a patch of sunshine, you’ll know it is using photosynthesis to produce clean air.

attacks.name: Assisting Heater
attacks.effect: Take a [R] Energy from your Energy Zone and attach it to 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

A3b-71.flavor_text: Inhaled air is carried to its flame sac, heated, and exhaled as fire that reaches over 3,000 degrees Fahrenheit.

attacks.name: Hyper Whirlpool
attacks.effect: Flip a coin until you get tails. For each heads, discard a random Energy from your opponent's Active Pokémon.

A3b-72.flavor_text: It lives close to water. Its long tail is ridged with a fin, which is often mistaken for a mermaid’s.

attacks.name: Ice Blade
attacks.effect: This attack does 50 damage to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon.

A3b-73.flavor_text: It can control its body temperature at will. This enables it to freeze the moisture in the atmosphere, creating flurries of diamond dust.

attacks.name: Beginning Bolt
attacks.effect: If this Pokémon evolved during this turn, this attack does 20 more damage.

A3b-74.flavor_text: It concentrates the weak electric charges emitted by its cells and launches wicked lightning bolts.

attacks.name: Energy Crush
attacks.effect: This attack does 20 damage for each Energy attached to all of your opponent's Pokémon.

A3b-75.flavor_text: The tip of its forked tail quivers when it is predicting its opponent’s next move.

attacks.name: Evoharmony
attacks.effect: This attack does 30 more damage for each Evolution Pokémon on your Bench.

A3b-76.flavor_text: This Pokémon uses its ribbonlike feelers to send a soothing aura into its opponents, erasing their hostility.

attacks.name: Dark Binding
attacks.effect: If the Defending Pokémon is a Basic Pokémon, it can't attack during your opponent's next turn.

A3b-77.flavor_text: When exposed to the moon’s aura, the rings on its body glow faintly and it gains a mysterious power.

attacks.name: Collect
attacks.effect: Draw a card.

A3b-78.flavor_text: Its ability to evolve into many forms allows it to adapt smoothly and perfectly to any environment.

attacks.name: Fire Spin
attacks.effect: Discard 2 [R] Energy from this Pokémon.

ability.name: Ability: Combust
ability.effect: Once during your turn, you may attach a [R] Energy from your discard pile to this Pokémon. If you do, do 20 damage to this Pokémon.

attacks.name: Hydro Pump
attacks.effect: If this Pokémon has at least 1 extra [W] Energy attached, this attack does 40 more damage.

attacks.name: Sparkling Aria
attacks.effect: Heal 20 damage from this Pokémon.

ability.name: Ability: Happy Ribbon
ability.effect: Once during your turn, when you play this Pokémon from your hand to evolve 1 of your Pokémon, you may draw 2 cards.

attacks.name: Giga Impact
attacks.effect: During your next turn, this Pokémon can't attack.

ability.effect: At the end of your turn, if this Pokémon is in the Active Spot, heal 20 damage from it.

ability.effect: Once during your turn, you may attach a [R] Energy from your discard pile to this Pokémon. If you do, do 20 damage to this Pokémon.

attacks.effect: If this Pokémon has at least 1 extra [W] Energy attached, this attack does 40 more damage.

attacks.effect: Heal 20 damage from this Pokémon.

ability.name: Ability: Happy Ribbon
ability.effect: Once during your turn, when you play this Pokémon from your hand to evolve 1 of your Pokémon, you may draw 2 cards.

attacks.effect: This Pokémon is now Asleep.
